programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: Invading aliens take over the Enterprise, but Spock discovers a surprisingly human chink in their seemingly invincible armour. Classic sci-fi adventure, starring William Shatner and Leonard Nimoy

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: Steve gets involved with moonshiners while investigating the case of two federal agents apparently killed by snake bites

programme.title: Babylon 5
programme.desc: G'Kar is terrified by an old enemy's threat, while Sinclair and Catherine rekindle their romance during a week-long festival celebrating universal spirituality

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Sydney and Gage go undercover as bikers to expose an illegal methamphetamine operation. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: Survivor (2015)
programme.desc: A US embassy official based in London goes on the run after being framed for a terrorist bombing, in which several of her colleagues are killed, and discovers a plot to detonate another bomb in Times Square during New Year's Eve celebrations in New York. Thriller, starring Milla Jovovich, Pierce Brosnan and Dylan McDermott

programme.title: Lawless (2012)
programme.desc: Three brothers run an underground bootlegging operation in Depression-era Virginia. However, as their criminal activities become increasingly successful, a corrupt deputy sheriff demands a cut of the profits, forcing them to fight to defend their livelihood. Crime drama, starring Shia LaBeouf, Tom Hardy, Jason Clarke, Gary Oldman and Guy Pearce

programme.title: Unthinkable (2010)
programme.desc: A trained black-ops interrogator and an FBI agent are forced to resort to extreme measures to coerce a rogue nuclear expert to divulge the locations of three devices ready to explode in major US cities. Gregor Jordan's thriller, starring Samuel L Jackson, Carrie-Anne Moss, Michael Sheen and Stephen Root

programme.title: The Trust (2016)
programme.desc: Two bumbling police officers working in the evidence room of a Las Vegas station are surprised to discover a bail receipt for an unusually high sum of money. They hatch a plan to find out where this money came from, and steal it for themselves. Comedy thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, Elijah Wood and Jerry Lewis

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 13-year-old aspiring Olympian fractures his pelvis after falling from his bike, an injury that may have devastating long-term consequences for his sporting career. As doctors determine if an operation is needed, his mother reflects on how a spinal injury brought an end to her own dreams when she was a teenager. A motorcyclist is brought in to St Georges, having been flung six feet in the air following a collision with a car

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: In Birmingham, paramedics Adi Ahmed and Mark McDonagh rush to a 10-year-old boy who has had a major epileptic fit. At the same time in Coventry, an elderly woman with heart and lung failure is struggling to breathe but refuses to go to hospital for treatment. Plus, in Warwick, a crew is called to an elderly man who has extreme anxiety and has called emergency services 14 times

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Three patients and how they cope when life takes an unexpected turn, including a patient who severed three of his fingers with an electric saw. Plus, a man with motor neurone disease is rushed to St George's with severe sepsis, and a teenager struggles to come to terms with the impact of serious injuries he sustained in a car crash six weeks ago

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An elderly woman is admitted with two severe leg fractures, with staff concerned she may not survive her injuries. Viktoria, her daughter, talks about Iris's life during the Blitz, and the touching story of her parents' romance that ended in tragedy. Elijah appears with an injured finger that he caught in a kebab slicer and 54-year-old Alison is rushed to A&E with sepsis, a life-threatening immune response to a chest infection

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to Kenya to see how cold-brew coffee differs from iced coffee. Helen Lawal tries frozen pastries in Portugal to see how well they perform well against fresh, while Briony May Williams heads to Staffordshire to learn how parsnips are cultivated to taste so sweet

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This competition takes place in and around Leeds, where the first to host is 24-year-old digital account manager Alice. Alice has never held a dinner party before, but she's hoping her family favourites will hit the spot and win her the £1,000 prize. She's hoping to impress Danielle, Andy, Lynne and Matt. With gob-smacking revelations, cringe-worthy party tricks and a ginormous Pavlova, can Alice manage to walk away with the honours?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Night two of the competition in Leeds, and company director Andy hopes to win with a menu of restaurant-inspired fare, but Alice and Danielle are more impressed with his Ferrari

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fun-loving student Danielle hosts the third dinner party in Leeds, hoping a high-calorie menu will see her emerge victorious. She even serves dessert wearing her wedding dress

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Theatre technician Matt is the fourth to host in Leeds with a menu of family favourites - and if that does not win them over, hopefully his juggling skills will do the trick

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition in and around Leeds, and jeweller Lynne hopes her night will sparkle with a menu consisting of her own creations. While talk at the table turns to plastic surgery and Botox, Andy and Danielle aren't too impressed with Lynne's food

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Danni Menzies arrives in the beautiful coastal town of Nerja in southern Spain to meet house-hunter Frances, who is dreaming of buying a holiday home she can enjoy with her friends and family. With her daughter Karina also on hand to lend her support, Frances would love to find a two-bed property close to the sea, so Danni sets out to deliver her dream within a budget of £200,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jo and Neil are about to start their next big adventure and move from their home in Exeter to start a new life in Lecce in southern Italy. They want a property that has a guest annex to accommodate friends and family, and that's close to the beach so Neil can surf whenever he wants. Armed with a budget of £200,000, Jasmine Harman shows them five properties for them to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps social worker Gilly and her husband Andy to find their dream home where they can permanently relocate. Andy proposed to Gilly in Portugal 26 years ago, and now the couple have decided that the countryside inland from the Silver Coast would be the perfect place to spend their retirement. With Andy unable to join the search, Gilly brings along her daughter Kat for support. They're looking for a property with four bedrooms, a garden and a pool for their budget of £210,000. Ben amasses five options, but will any of them hit the right note and result in an offer?

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George visits Manchester to bring an arts and crafts-inspired Edwardian home into the modern day. In London, he meets Flo, a first-time buyer whose flat needs a rejig to make it the sociable space she has always wanted

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer visit the Payne-Durkins, on the rural outskirts of Leeds, to help Karl and Paul decide whether to renovate or sell their three-bedroom home. Karl loves the unique design and idyllic location, but Paul thinks they are too isolated, and would rather move to another area than spend the moving improving their current home

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Beverly has enjoyed a 30-year love affair with all things Italian, and now can't wait to relocate to the spectacular countryside and medieval towns of Umbria in Italy, to indulge her twin passions of history and baking. Beverley and her mother, Margaret, are looking for a rural house with a garden, and space for her family to visit on a budget of £150,000. Property expert Sara Damergi shows the pair five options to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired NHS workers Rachel and John cannot wait to buy their very own holiday home in the town of Oliva on the stunning Valencian coastline. They're keen cyclists and beekeepers and want a home with a garden that works for them all year around, and that can also accommodate their five adult children, who plan to visit regularly. With a budget of £100,000, Ben Hillman shows the pair five options to choose between

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: This week's competition kicks off at The Red Lion in Charing Heath, Kent, where the owners treat the other hosts to a masterclass in hospitality at nearby Leeds Castle

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition continues in Battle in East Sussex, at Dean and Becky's Glamp in Style campsite. The hosts have novelty on their side, with their unique accommodation including a converted army truck, a horsebox and a vintage bus but not everyone is keen to embrace the alternative glamping experience. In the afternoon, Becky lets loose with a saucy tale amid the local fauna and flora and at feedback time, cold water leaves the hosts feeling the heat

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Best friends Helen and Phil host the week's third visit at Tottington Manor in West Sussex. Sam and Claudia are bowled over by the decor, but some guests raise their eyebrows at the high prices. An afternoon at a local rum distillery makes Helen and Phil the toast of the town. The hosts can't stop smiling when they run through their feedback - until some comments right at the end spoil things and give Helen an axe to grind for payment day

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sam and Claudia host the week's final visit at their self-catering Lakeview Studios in Dorset. The digs get approving eyes from Claire and Lee, but Dean and Becky's arrival is marked by a problem with their toilet. Ahead of the all-important payment day, battle lines are drawn at a tank museum

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment Day promises to be a fiery affair after two sets of B&Bers in particular participated in tit-for-tat marking throughout the week. Eventually, the week's winner is announced

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: The fifth series of the show following the doctors and nurses at The Royal Melbourne Hospital. Time is critical as Dr Scott Taylor's unresponsive patient deteriorates with a suspected brain bleed, and Dr Tristan Harding must straighten a young man's dislocated ankle

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Fifty-four-year-old James is rushed to A&E after a head-on collision with his car windscreen, leaving doctors concerned he may have sustained a serious life changing injury. Justice waits with his girlfriend Elissa to have a worrying lump on his finger examined

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: Dr Mark Putland examines a quarry worker's mangled arm, and Dr Jonathan Papson carefully removes a screw from a patient's thumb after a DIY mishap

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: The fifth series of the show following the doctors and nurses at The Royal Melbourne Hospital. Time is critical as Dr Scott Taylor's unresponsive patient deteriorates with a suspected brain bleed, and Dr Tristan Harding must straighten a young man's dislocated ankle

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Fifty-four-year-old James is rushed to A&E after a head-on collision with his car windscreen, leaving doctors concerned he may have sustained a serious life changing injury. Justice waits with his girlfriend Elissa to have a worrying lump on his finger examined

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 13-year-old aspiring Olympian fractures his pelvis after falling from his bike, an injury that may have devastating long-term consequences for his sporting career. As doctors determine if an operation is needed, his mother reflects on how a spinal injury brought an end to her own dreams when she was a teenager. A motorcyclist is brought in to St Georges, having been flung six feet in the air following a collision with a car

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: In Lincolnshire, Kate Quilton sees whether shallots are actually just small onions and visits a potato farm looking for the reason baking potatoes cost more. Matt Tebbutt heads to frozen food company McCain to see how potatoes are transformed into their various products, most notably chips. Helen Lawal is in Spain to find out why it is possible to get sheep's cheese in the supermarket, but never sheep's milk

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The researchers investigate the case of a famous Blackpool accordion player, and reveal a tragic tale of separation involving two brothers who died on the same day in the same hospital after not seeing each other for many years. Plus, the team explores the history of Rolls-Royce and the Crewe railways, before sharing details of other unclaimed estates

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole are in north Wales to meet Emma, whose father ran a haulage and motor repair business, to see what unloved items they can restore

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Spain, where Ben Eyre and Anna Chalu are first to host at Vista Del Torcal in the Andalusian Mountains. Despite being impressed with their rooms, some guests are less enthused by an afternoon of rock climbing, leaving Ben and Anna hoping a full English breakfast will save their bacon

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jed and Mandy Filmer welcome their rivals to the Hotel la Tartana on Spain's Costa Tropical, and hope to impress them with a cookery class - not realising one of their guests is a qualified chef. A revelation at dinner stuns the group, before the Filmers' attitude to breakfast service jeopardises their chances of victory

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Julia Grant hosts the third round of the competition in Spain at her Benidorm B&B The Queens Hotel. Her guests are not impressed with her standards of cleanliness, finding dirt, dust and hairs, and tensions rise at dinner over her 'straight-friendly' label. The owner improves the evening by giving everyone a drag makeover and persuading them to perform in a revue, but some of the feedback she receives leaves her enraged

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's final hosts in Spain are Alastair Smith and Martyn Watson, owners of the gay and naturist-friendly Almond Tree Villa in Murcia. The day's activity is olive picking, but the mood turns sour when Jed admits he gave Julia's hotel low marks, while a disaster in the bathroom and a sugar-rich continental breakfast reflect badly on Alastair and Martyn's feedback scores

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants of the Spanish special meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition comes from in and around Belfast, where the first host is chatty PR manager Claire. She aims to impress with a menu made up of her favourite restaurant dishes, but never having plucked a pheasant before, she has a challenge on her hands. The guests she's hoping to impress are DJ Ryan, joiner and part-time inventor Neil, charity events manager Cailin, and window cleaning manager Paul, on a lively night filled with laughter, music and dancing

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Carpenter Neil hosts the second dinner party in and around Belfast in a bid to win the £1000 prize, presenting good old fashioned, country style cuisine to his guests. With a farmyard full of animals joining in the fun, discussions ranging from disabled chickens to eating stinging nettles and a guest performance from Neil's very own creation, ''head in a jar', it's proves to be a weird and wonderful experience out in the countryside

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Debonair window cleaning business owner and keen golfer Paul has cut a dash already this week in and around Belfast with his sense of style, and now he hopes to impress with his fine food. For Ryan and Neil, most of the fancy fare is uncharted territory, and while Cailin and Claire are impressed with the fine dining, Paul sails close to the wind when he suggests that they might like to be his ladies-in-waiting

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Livewire DJ and events organiser Ryan is the next to host in and around Belfast - and having arranged parties for thousands, is confident he can satisfy four fellow diners. Eccentric joiner Neil anticipates a madcap evening of fun, and with a hipster band playing in the living room, unexpected discoveries in the bedroom and several crises in the kitchen, certainly gets the entertainment he was hoping for

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night in Belfast, Cailin hosts a Eurovision extravaganza. Her rivals have provided top-notch nosh and entertainment, but she believes that her tried-and-tested dishes from across Europe will land her the top spot

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the Bowling Green in Chorlton-cum-Hardy, Manchester, where owner Mark Canny hopes his newly refurbished rooms and head for hosting will secure him success. Guests James and Fiona rave about their room, but Martin and Petra find a few furnishing faults. At dinner, Mark gets his guests giggling, while Andrew and Qidi's revelation that they're big hitters in the five-star hotel world sends everyone's expectations sky high. In the morning, Mark's Irish breakfast gratifies the guests, but a fridge feud leaves him far from chilled

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Redhill Holidays in Whitbourne near Worcester, a glamping site with pole-perched premier pods - and an unwelcome population of insects

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week comes from the Mode Lytham Hotel in Lytham, Lancashire, where husband-and-wife team Andrew and Qidi Whitaker hope their hotel will bag them best in show

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Villa Farm Cottage in Thornton Curtis in Lincolnshire, where perfectionist Petra Coates and her partner Martin Littlefield run a tight ship. When the guests arrive, the bathroom tiles take Andrew back to his public swimming pool days and Mark has a moan about a missing en suite. In the morning, the guests are spoilt for choice as Petra provides a German cold meat feast followed by an English breakfast cooked by Martin. However, Andrew and Qidi's feedback about the tiling leaves Martin and Petra planning to hit back on payment day

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and settle some scores. Mark from the Bowling Green Hotel questions Martin and Petra about a lack of chairs and then gives Qidi and Andrew's noisy fridge comments a frosty reception

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer visit the Great British seaside in Kent to search for properties for two sets of buyers. Kirstie is searching for a home for a family of six, who had the perfect property but lost it due to a clerical error, while Phil helps a family from London as they search for an idyllic seaside lifestyle

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce challenges music fans from across the country to recall facts and stats about their favourite chart toppers and beyond, from the 1950s to the present day. In this first heat match, Sophie from Milton Keynes, Matt from Bristol, Beth from Crewe, Melanie from Beverley and Kuresh from London compete over six rounds in the hope of securing a place in the Grand Final

programme.title: Britain's Railway Empire in Colour
programme.desc: To mark the 200th anniversary of the birth of rail, this series reveals the story of how Britain gave railways to the world - from the first tentative experiments with steam power, through the domestic boom years, to the exporting of this transformative new technology to all corners of the globe. Experts and enthusiasts and extensive newly colourised archive explore the truly revolutionary nature - both good and bad - of the locomotive. The first episode tracks how the railways grew from coal trucks in the north-east of England to transforming the lives of the Victorians. Trainspotter Francis Bourgeois tells the story of his 19th-century equivalent, schoolboy George Tod, who took stunning black and white photographs of his favourite engines. These and other photographs have been colourised, bringing the old railways back to life. There are also stories from the Empire as well as narratives from closer to home, including the magnificent Forth Bridge

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The hospital documentary returns, focusing on the stories of two motorcyclists, which reveal how the bonds between loved ones can be strengthened through adversity. Prezmek may have life-changing spinal injuries following a high-speed motorcycle crash on the M4, forcing his wife Tatia to reflect on how their plans for the future may have to change. Teenager Leon undergoes a CT scan after crashing into a central reservation. While waiting for the results, his mother Janey thinks back to his school days

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: In Coventry, crewmates Mark Rattray and Orlaigh Barbour attend a 73-year-old woman who is in agony after falling on the ice. Jan Vann and Chelsie Kennedy are sent to a baby who has been fitting. On the other side of the city, Neil Vann and Laura Perkins have to deal with a well-known drunk nuisance caller

programme.title: Britain's Railway Empire in Colour
programme.desc: To mark the 200th anniversary of the birth of rail, this series reveals the story of how Britain gave railways to the world - from the first tentative experiments with steam power, through the domestic boom years, to the exporting of this transformative new technology to all corners of the globe. Experts and enthusiasts and extensive newly colourised archive explore the truly revolutionary nature - both good and bad - of the locomotive. The first episode tracks how the railways grew from coal trucks in the north-east of England to transforming the lives of the Victorians. Trainspotter Francis Bourgeois tells the story of his 19th-century equivalent, schoolboy George Tod, who took stunning black and white photographs of his favourite engines. These and other photographs have been colourised, bringing the old railways back to life. There are also stories from the Empire as well as narratives from closer to home, including the magnificent Forth Bridge

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The hospital documentary returns, focusing on the stories of two motorcyclists, which reveal how the bonds between loved ones can be strengthened through adversity. Prezmek may have life-changing spinal injuries following a high-speed motorcycle crash on the M4, forcing his wife Tatia to reflect on how their plans for the future may have to change. Teenager Leon undergoes a CT scan after crashing into a central reservation. While waiting for the results, his mother Janey thinks back to his school days

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Karan is rushed to St George's by ambulance after being tackled in a football match, leaving the 24-year-old with fractured bones in his leg. Will has a painful swelling on his head due to an infection of his scalp. The 30-year-old shares the story of how his teenage life was suddenly altered after an unprovoked assault. He describes how his brain injury was life-changing, but that it's totally altered his outlook on life. Rock music fan Jonathan suffers a broken toe after dropping a wooden chopping board on his foot

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: The fifth series of the show following the doctors and nurses at The Royal Melbourne Hospital. Time is critical as Dr Scott Taylor's unresponsive patient deteriorates with a suspected brain bleed, and Dr Tristan Harding must straighten a young man's dislocated ankle

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Three patients and how they cope when life takes an unexpected turn, including a patient who severed three of his fingers with an electric saw. Plus, a man with motor neurone disease is rushed to St George's with severe sepsis, and a teenager struggles to come to terms with the impact of serious injuries he sustained in a car crash six weeks ago

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An elderly woman is admitted with two severe leg fractures, with staff concerned she may not survive her injuries. Viktoria, her daughter, talks about Iris's life during the Blitz, and the touching story of her parents' romance that ended in tragedy. Elijah appears with an injured finger that he caught in a kebab slicer and 54-year-old Alison is rushed to A&E with sepsis, a life-threatening immune response to a chest infection

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to Kenya to see how cold-brew coffee differs from iced coffee. Helen Lawal tries frozen pastries in Portugal to see how well they perform well against fresh, while Briony May Williams heads to Staffordshire to learn how parsnips are cultivated to taste so sweet

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This competition takes place in and around Leeds, where the first to host is 24-year-old digital account manager Alice. Alice has never held a dinner party before, but she's hoping her family favourites will hit the spot and win her the £1,000 prize. She's hoping to impress Danielle, Andy, Lynne and Matt. With gob-smacking revelations, cringe-worthy party tricks and a ginormous Pavlova, can Alice manage to walk away with the honours?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Night two of the competition in Leeds, and company director Andy hopes to win with a menu of restaurant-inspired fare, but Alice and Danielle are more impressed with his Ferrari

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fun-loving student Danielle hosts the third dinner party in Leeds, hoping a high-calorie menu will see her emerge victorious. She even serves dessert wearing her wedding dress

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Theatre technician Matt is the fourth to host in Leeds with a menu of family favourites - and if that does not win them over, hopefully his juggling skills will do the trick

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition in and around Leeds, and jeweller Lynne hopes her night will sparkle with a menu consisting of her own creations. While talk at the table turns to plastic surgery and Botox, Andy and Danielle aren't too impressed with Lynne's food

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Danni Menzies arrives in the beautiful coastal town of Nerja in southern Spain to meet house-hunter Frances, who is dreaming of buying a holiday home she can enjoy with her friends and family. With her daughter Karina also on hand to lend her support, Frances would love to find a two-bed property close to the sea, so Danni sets out to deliver her dream within a budget of £200,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jo and Neil are about to start their next big adventure and move from their home in Exeter to start a new life in Lecce in southern Italy. They want a property that has a guest annex to accommodate friends and family, and that's close to the beach so Neil can surf whenever he wants. Armed with a budget of £200,000, Jasmine Harman shows them five properties for them to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps social worker Gilly and her husband Andy to find their dream home where they can permanently relocate. Andy proposed to Gilly in Portugal 26 years ago, and now the couple have decided that the countryside inland from the Silver Coast would be the perfect place to spend their retirement. With Andy unable to join the search, Gilly brings along her daughter Kat for support. They're looking for a property with four bedrooms, a garden and a pool for their budget of £210,000. Ben amasses five options, but will any of them hit the right note and result in an offer?

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George visits Manchester to bring an arts and crafts-inspired Edwardian home into the modern day. In London, he meets Flo, a first-time buyer whose flat needs a rejig to make it the sociable space she has always wanted

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer visit the Payne-Durkins, on the rural outskirts of Leeds, to help Karl and Paul decide whether to renovate or sell their three-bedroom home. Karl loves the unique design and idyllic location, but Paul thinks they are too isolated, and would rather move to another area than spend the moving improving their current home

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Beverly has enjoyed a 30-year love affair with all things Italian, and now can't wait to relocate to the spectacular countryside and medieval towns of Umbria in Italy, to indulge her twin passions of history and baking. programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and settle some scores. Mark from the Bowling Green Hotel questions Martin and Petra about a lack of chairs and then gives Qidi and Andrew's noisy fridge comments a frosty reception

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer visit the Great British seaside in Kent to search for properties for two sets of buyers. Kirstie is searching for a home for a family of six, who had the perfect property but lost it due to a clerical error, while Phil helps a family from London as they search for an idyllic seaside lifestyle

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce challenges music fans from across the country to recall facts and stats about their favourite chart toppers and beyond, from the 1950s to the present day. In this first heat match, Sophie from Milton Keynes, Matt from Bristol, Beth from Crewe, Melanie from Beverley and Kuresh from London compete over six rounds in the hope of securing a place in the Grand Final

programme.title: Britain's Railway Empire in Colour
programme.desc: To mark the 200th anniversary of the birth of rail, this series reveals the story of how Britain gave railways to the world - from the first tentative experiments with steam power, through the domestic boom years, to the exporting of this transformative new technology to all corners of the globe. Experts and enthusiasts and extensive newly colourised archive explore the truly revolutionary nature - both good and bad - of the locomotive. The first episode tracks how the railways grew from coal trucks in the north-east of England to transforming the lives of the Victorians. Trainspotter Francis Bourgeois tells the story of his 19th-century equivalent, schoolboy George Tod, who took stunning black and white photographs of his favourite engines. These and other photographs have been colourised, bringing the old railways back to life. There are also stories from the Empire as well as narratives from closer to home, including the magnificent Forth Bridge

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The hospital documentary returns, focusing on the stories of two motorcyclists, which reveal how the bonds between loved ones can be strengthened through adversity. Prezmek may have life-changing spinal injuries following a high-speed motorcycle crash on the M4, forcing his wife Tatia to reflect on how their plans for the future may have to change. Teenager Leon undergoes a CT scan after crashing into a central reservation. While waiting for the results, his mother Janey thinks back to his school days

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: Dr Mark Putland examines a quarry worker's mangled arm, and Dr Jonathan Papson carefully removes a screw from a patient's thumb after a DIY mishap

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Karan is rushed to St George's by ambulance after being tackled in a football match, leaving the 24-year-old with fractured bones in his leg. Will has a painful swelling on his head due to an infection of his scalp. The 30-year-old shares the story of how his teenage life was suddenly altered after an unprovoked assault. He describes how his brain injury was life-changing, but that it's totally altered his outlook on life. Rock music fan Jonathan suffers a broken toe after dropping a wooden chopping board on his foot

programme.desc: New Orleans is flooded after the levees breach. Residents try to evacuate, rescue efforts begin, and a chaotic atmosphere sets in

programme.title: Hurricane Katrina: Race Against Time
programme.desc: Three days into the flood, rescue operations continue around the clock. Thousands remain trapped, as the government struggles to coordinate a rescue effort

programme.title: Hurricane Katrina: Race Against Time
programme.desc: In post-Katrina New Orleans, General Honoré leads rescue efforts amid pressure to use force, while local heroes tirelessly save lives

programme.title: Hurricane Katrina: Race Against Time
programme.desc: The future of a devastated New Orleans hangs in the balance as its residents struggle to return, rebuild and restore their lives even 20 years later

programme.title: Hidden Beneath the Cities
programme.desc: Poland's salt mine, a Cold War bunker, an ancient Egyptian site, and China's mysterious Longyou Caves.

programme.title: Engineering by Catastrophe

programme.title: Inside the CIA: Secrets and Spies

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation

programme.title: Toronto Airport Uncovered

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw restore a Citroën SM - widely considered one of the greatest French cars ever - after it suffered catastrophic engine failure on its first outing

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw head to Norfolk to take on a 1962 TVR Grantura that has been off the road for nearly 50 years

programme.title: Megafactories
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at an American crisp factory, following workers during the build-up to the 4th of July celebrations, when the plant uses a million pounds of potatoes a day

programme.title: Megafactories
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at Porsche's revamped factory in Leipzig, where the four-door Panamera model is built on a single assembly line tended by 600 workers. programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary capturing the changing seasons and the life cycles of a range of wildlife in Spain and Portugal's forests, a region of great diversity. Spring and summer in the woodlands see the breeding season in full swing, with some eyecatching skills help ensure their genetic replication and aid them in overcoming constant challenges.

programme.title: Iberia's Woodlands: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: Part two of two. Autumn and winter in the Mediterranean forests see new offspring having to learn to fend for themselves, while inter-species rivalry is at a peak, and constant challenges sometimes culminate in bloody skirmishes

programme.title: Europe from Above
programme.desc: Aerial footage of European countries, beginning with the UK, including glimpses of the Giant's Causeway, the Eden Project, and one of London's tallest skyscrapers

programme.title: Europe from Above
programme.desc: This aerial tour of the islands of Great Britain reveals a picturesque beach airport, an oil rig recycling operation and a dramatic Viking fire festival

programme.title: United States of Animals

programme.title: Blitzkrieg Sharks
programme.desc: Documentary exploring extraordinary predatory shark behaviour, which seems to reverse conventional thinking on what sharks eat, and where. Remains of song birds have been found in the stomachs of tiger sharks in the Gulf of Mexico, prompting wildlife researchers to speculate whether the boundaries of shore and air are no longer safe. The film also highlights why sharks are beaching themselves in search of prey in French Frigate Shoals in the northwest Hawaiian Islands

programme.desc: Documentary following the chimpanzees at a sanctuary in Sierra Leone as they seek to find their place within the troop

programme.title: Border Protection Spain
programme.desc: Danger comes on jet skis on the east coast, bales of hashish are hidden beneath the waves, and attempts are made to track the signal of a narco-submarine

programme.title: To Catch a Smuggler
programme.desc: In Puerto Rico, AMO agents pursue a sailing vessel that is making an attempt to leave with cocaine valued at $2.5 million

programme.title: Dutch Caribbean Coastguards
programme.desc: The Search and Rescue helicopter searches for a missing girl on Bonaire, and the Coastguards receive a call to save the driver of a sinking water scooter

programme.title: Last of the Giants: Wild Fish
programme.desc: Cyril Chauquet sets his sights on the African cubera, the biggest snapper on the planet, whose massive tail and sheer strength make landing one no easy task

programme.title: The Incredible Dr Pol
programme.desc: On Mother's Day, the vets treat new mums including a cow with a young calf. Plus, a pair of dogs need help after a painful encounter with a porcupine

programme.title: Yukon Vet
programme.desc: The Oakleys are back at the Reindeer Farm, but this time the herd is in rut and their raging hormones are making them aggressive and unruly

programme.title: Savage Kingdom
programme.desc: As drought turns Mombo to dust, Phefo the leopard must travel for food, but with their mother gone, the cubs are trapped by merciless killers

programme.title: Plunderer: The Life and Times of a Nazi Art Thief
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jonathan Petropoulos investigates Nazi art dealer Bruno Lohse's life in post-war America. Dealing in looted art, he became an invisible hand, even as the original owners' families sought the return of works rightfully theirs. Featuring an archive interviews with Lohse himself, as well as curators, investigators and descendants of the victims of Nazi art theft

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: For his final tour, Paul Murton goes over the sea to Skye, where he visits the home of writer and naturalist Gavin Maxwell, before scaling the famous Cuillin mountain, Am Basteir. On the nearby Isle of Raasay he learns the amazing true story of the crofter Calum Macleod, who between 1964 and 1974 singlehandedly built a road to connect his isolated community

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.sub-title: Life at the Ends of the Earth
programme.desc: Paul Murton returns to visit more islands off the coastline, beginning with the Outer Hebrides. He resumes his journey on Eriskay, where he discovers how the remote location helped preserve a unique way of life for centuries - and also allowed locals to hide thousands of bottles of whisky from the excise men when a ship laden with the thirst-quenching cargo ran aground in 1941. The story inspired the 1949 movie Whisky Galore! Moving on to Barra, Paul goes foraging for cockles and visits the only airport in the world where scheduled flights land on the beach, then heads across to Vatersay, where he joins local fishermen trawling for lobsters

programme.title: Battlefield America
programme.desc: Charting the pivotal battles of the American Civil War, underscoring their impact on the broader conflict and on American history. Beginning by examining the strategic decision made by Union and Confederate leaders and the intense combat experienced by soldiers at the battles of Shiloh and Antietam. Part one of three

programme.title: Battlefield America
programme.desc: Highlighting the strategic decisions of Ulysses S. Grant and Robert E. Lee and the intense combat that defined the crucial battles of Vicksburg and Gettysburg, underscoring their impact on the broader conflict and American history. Part two of three

programme.title: Battlefield America
programme.desc: Showcasing the intense fighting and strategic errors in the Battle of Chickamauga that led to significant losses on both sides. Plus, a look at General Grant's Overland Campaign, a series of battles aimed at defeating General Lee's forces. Part three of three

programme.title: The Civil War's Lost Massacre
programme.desc: The search for remains of formerly enslaved Black Union soldiers brutally murdered by angry Southerners as the Civil War was ending. Plus, a deep dive into Camp Nelson, Kentucky, the military base where these soldiers and thousands of others got their freedom papers

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.sub-title: Life at the Ends of the Earth
programme.desc: Paul Murton returns to visit more islands off the coastline, beginning with the Outer Hebrides. He resumes his journey on Eriskay, where he discovers how the remote location helped preserve a unique way of life for centuries - and also allowed locals to hide thousands of bottles of whisky from the excise men when a ship laden with the thirst-quenching cargo ran aground in 1941. The story inspired the 1949 movie Whisky Galore! Moving on to Barra, Paul goes foraging for cockles and visits the only airport in the world where scheduled flights land on the beach, then heads across to Vatersay, where he joins local fishermen trawling for lobsters

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians take a trip on the Flying Scotsman, arguably the most famous locomotive in the world, along its original route connecting London and Edinburgh - the two most important financial capitals of the Empire. Peter Ginn explores how the railways revolutionised the delivery of mail across Britain, he and Alex Langlands learn about the first electronic communication service - more than 100 years before the arrival of email - and Ruth finds out about the role of the wheeltapper. Plus, why do some railway clocks have more than one minute hand?

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians explore the life of the South Devon branch line before the Beeching cuts of the 1960s. Peter Ginn joins a footplate crew and receives coal-shovelling tips from a veteran steam engine driver of 63 years' experience, and with Alex he learns about one of Scotland's most lucrative exports. Ruth Goodman finds out about the dangers involved in working on the Victorian railways, and how those who lost limbs were catered for by a dedicated prosthetics workshop. She also discovers how the railways came to the rescue when a deadly disease wiped out almost the entire stock of London cattle

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The team discover how the combination of increased leisure time and affordable rail transport brought a new kind of freedom for working-class Victorians. Ruth Goodman travels along the south Devon coast from Paignton to Kingswear, where she helps prepare a paddle steamer for a journey up the River Dart, while at Swanage, Peter Ginn finds out what it was like to work on the excursion trains and the impact mass tourism had on the area

programme.title: Constantine the Great
programme.desc: The first in a two-part profile of the ancient Roman emperor, as archaeologists plot the different stages of his life from Naissus to Rome

programme.title: Constantine the Great
programme.desc: The concluding episode follows the emperor's archaeological traces to the new Rome, Constantinople, where his claim to power knows no bounds

programme.title: Battlefield America
programme.desc: Charting the pivotal battles of the American Civil War, underscoring their impact on the broader conflict and on American history. programme.title: The Civil War's Lost Massacre
programme.desc: The search for remains of formerly enslaved Black Union soldiers brutally murdered by angry Southerners as the Civil War was ending. Plus, a deep dive into Camp Nelson, Kentucky, the military base where these soldiers and thousands of others got their freedom papers

programme.title: Cheating Hitler: Surviving the Holocaust
programme.desc: Three Canadian Holocaust survivors journey to home towns, killing sites, archives and hiding places in search of clues to unanswered questions from their pasts. Maxwell wonders what happened to a baby he saved in a forest in 1943, Helen wants to know more about the fate of her brother, while Rose is retracing the final steps of the woman who saved her life. The survivors came of age during the Holocaust and carry the burden of knowing they are the last living link to it

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton explores the Isle of Mull and its satellite islands to discover why they have become boltholes from the hustle and bustle of the modern world. He begins on the tidal island of Erraid, which inspired famous Scottish writer Robert Louis Stevenson, then hops over to Ulva, the final resting place of Lachlan MacQuarrie, one of the first governors of New South Wales. He then explores Mull itself, being given a whistlestop tour of the island by rally driver Louise Thomas, then travels to the mysterious little island of Inch Kenneth, where he hears the story of Nazi sympathizer Unity Mitford. He ends his tour at the remote Treshnish Islands, where he experiences a spot of 'puffin therapy'

programme.title: Alexandria: The Greatest City
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes examines the history of the ancient city, which was founded by Alexander the Great in 331 BC. Until its decline in the fourth and fifth centuries AD, it became the centre of the world of art and culture and an example of advanced civilisation

programme.title: Cheating Hitler: Surviving the Holocaust
programme.desc: Three Canadian Holocaust survivors journey to home towns, killing sites, archives and hiding places in search of clues to unanswered questions from their pasts. Maxwell wonders what happened to a baby he saved in a forest in 1943, Helen wants to know more about the fate of her brother, while Rose is retracing the final steps of the woman who saved her life. The survivors came of age during the Holocaust and carry the burden of knowing they are the last living link to it

programme.title: Alexandria: The Greatest City
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes examines the history of the ancient city, which was founded by Alexander the Great in 331 BC. Until its decline in the fourth and fifth centuries AD, it became the centre of the world of art and culture and an example of advanced civilisation

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton explores the Isle of Mull and its satellite islands to discover why they have become boltholes from the hustle and bustle of the modern world. He begins on the tidal island of Erraid, which inspired famous Scottish writer Robert Louis Stevenson, then hops over to Ulva, the final resting place of Lachlan MacQuarrie, one of the first governors of New South Wales. He then explores Mull itself, being given a whistlestop tour of the island by rally driver Louise Thomas, then travels to the mysterious little island of Inch Kenneth, where he hears the story of Nazi sympathizer Unity Mitford. He ends his tour at the remote Treshnish Islands, where he experiences a spot of 'puffin therapy'

programme.title: Titans of the Cold War
programme.desc: Charting the geopolitical struggle between the United States and the Soviet Union through the eyes of influential leaders from the end of the Second World War to the Cold War, showing how ego, ambition and paranoia collided. After the turbulent years following the Second World War, fragile peace gives way to rising tensions between former allies

programme.title: Le Mans 24 Hours
programme.desc: Concluding coverage from the third round of the FIA World Endurance Championship season, which takes place at Circuit de la Sarthe in Le Mans, France. This prestigious race, first held in 1923, is the oldest endurance event in the world, and is one of three races in the 'triple crown' alongside the Formula 1 Monaco Grand Prix and the Indycar Indianapolis 500. Legendary British driver Graham Hill is the only driver to complete the triple crown, while Fernando Alonso only needs to win the Indianapolis 500 to add his name to history

programme.title: Drop the Dead Donkey
programme.desc: GlobeLink News is taken over by Sir Roysten Merchant, and the staff worry about the intentions of their new chief executive, Gus Hedges. Comedy, starring Neil Pearson

programme.title: Drop the Dead Donkey

programme.title: Come Play With Me (1977)
programme.desc: A nurse at a health farm takes over running the establishment, and comes up with her own range of sexual treatments. Meanwhile, two forgers on the run hide out at the farm. Sex comedy, starring Mary Millington, Alfie Bass and Irene Handl

programme.title: Stereo Underground
programme.desc: Richard Latto presents alternative anthems from a music community with attitude.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Bullying, womanising and widely despised, Colonel James Bohun is found outside his brother's church with his skull smashed, leaving Father Brown to uncover the truth behind his violent end.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Ivory Coast v Ecuador (Kick-off 12.00am). Coverage of the Group E match at Philadelphia Stadium in the USA. This will be the first-ever meeting between these nations, and both will be aiming to get their campaigns off to a winning start having performed admirably in qualifying for this tournament. Les Éléphants finished atop their group in the CAF region after finishing with a record of eight wins and two draws from their 10 matches, while La Tricolor finished second in the highly competitive CONMEBOL region, trailing only Argentina, even after they were deducted three points related to an infringement from a previous World Cup qualification cycle. Neither of these nations has a stellar World Cup record from previous appearances, with Ivory Coast never having progressed beyond the group stage, and not having qualified since 2014, while Ecuador have appeared at four World Cup finals, most recently four years ago, making it to the last-16 on just one occasion

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Lacey tells John that she doesn't want to work for Tane because he broke her sister's heart. Sonny and Dana throw a birthday party for Archie

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Sonny is desperate to speed up his recovery, Lacey decides to help with the gym, and Roo is uncertain about Alf's innocence

programme.title: Up All Night
programme.desc: Adding to the fun around the 2026 FIFA World Cup, this vodcast is fronted by The Young Offenders stars Dominic MacHale and Shane Casey. With so many overnight kick-off times, the pair will stay up so viewers at home don't have to, bringing their own brand of Cork-flavoured chaos to football fans every morning with fresh news from the night before

programme.title: The Sunday Game Live

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Germany v Curaçao (Kick-off 6.00pm). Peter Collins presents coverage of the Group E match at Houston Stadium in the USA. The Caribbean nation face one of football's most decorated nations in their first-ever World Cup finals match as they go up against the four-time champions, who have also reached the final on four other occasions. Since their most recent World Cup victory at the 2014 tournament in Brazil, Die Mannschaft have failed to progress from the group stage and have only won two matches at the last two tournaments combined. Julian Nagelsmann's men came through qualifying with a record of five wins and one loss so will be confident of performing better this time around, but will be wary of an opponent they know little about having never faced them before, but who went undefeated in two stages of qualifying in the CONCACAF region

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Netherlands v Japan (Kick-off 9.00pm). Peter Collins presents coverage of the Group F match at Dallas Stadium in the USA. There have been three previous meetings between these nations, including a group-stage match at the 2010 World Cup in South Africa when a second-half goal from Wesley Sneijder was enough to give the Dutch a 1-0 victory. Both teams went on to progress to the knockout stage, and while the Japanese would bow out in the last-16, Oranje made it all the way to the final but fell short of victory in a 1-0 loss to Spain. Both teams were present at the previous World Cup in Qatar, and both topped their respective groups, with the Dutch leading Group A ahead of Senegal, Ecuador, and the host nation, while Samurai Blue bested Spain, Germany and Costa Rica. The most recent clash between tonight's opponents was a friendly match in 2013 that took place in Belgium, which saw Japan salvage a 2-2 draw after being 2-0 down

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Spain v Cape Verde (Kick-off 5.00pm). Peter Collins presents coverage of the opening match in Group H, held at Atlanta Stadium in the USA. Spain won this tournament in 2010 and are among the favourites to do so once again, having won Euro 2024 and qualified from a group containing Türkiye, Georgia and Bulgaria with five wins, a draw and no defeats. They were also runners-up to Portugal in last year's UEFA Nations League, and their squad boasts the talents of the likes of Barcelona stars Pedri and Lamine Yamal, who is an injury doubt for the group stage, and Manchester City's 2024 Ballon d'or winner Rodri. Cape Verde are one of the tournament outsiders and it would be a major upset should they manage to get a result this evening, but they impressed in qualifying, topping a group that contained the likes of Cameroon, Libya and Angola. They have few players among their ranks who would be widely considered as household names, but are likely to look to captain and all-time leading goalscorer Ryan Mendes for inspiration

Peter Collins presents as Group G gets under way with this fixture at Seattle Stadium in the USA, between two sides who will expect to progress to the knockout stages of the expanded competition. Iran and New Zealand are the other nations in this group and, while Belgium may seem to be a little past their best, following the relative failures of their 'Golden Generation', they still possess a squad that can compare with most of the entrants. The vast majority of the Egyptian squad ply their trade in their homeland, but two notable exceptions to that can be found in their attacking threat, in Liverpool's Mo Salah, and Omar Marmoush of Manchester City. Salah made his full international debut as far back as 2011, and scored twice at the 2018 World Cup and, despite his form and fortunes waning for his club this season, he still remains the dangerman as far as the Belgian defence is concerned

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Saudi Arabia v Uruguay (Kick-off 11.00pm). All the action from both teams' first match in Group H, held at Miami Stadium in the USA. The Saudis caused an upset against South American opposition in their opener four years ago in Qatar when they prevailed 2-1 against eventual champions Argentina, but defeats at the hands of Poland and Mexico ensured that 1994 remained the only time they had progressed beyond the group stage, when the tournament was also held in the USA. Uruguay lifted the trophy in 1930 and 1950, but their run of three consecutive appearances in the knockout stage came to an end in 2022, when they failed to make it out of a group containing Portugal, South Korea and Ghana. programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Haiti v Scotland (Kick-off 2.00am). Clare MacNamara presents coverage of the Group C match at Boston Stadium in the USA. Scotland are appearing at a World Cup finals for the first time since France '98, and their eighth overall, having finished top of a qualifying group that contained Denmark, Greece and Belarus. This will be their first-ever meeting with Haiti, who had to come through two phases of qualification to reach the finals for the second time in their history, with the previous appearance being in 1974, in West Germany. They will hope to perform better than they did on that occasion, when they lost three from three and conceded 14 goals, so will welcome an opener against an opponent that has only won four World Cup finals matches in their history, the most recent of which was a 2-1 win over Sweden at Italia '90

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Australia v Türkiye (Kick-off 5.00am). Coverage of the Group D match at BC Place Vancouver in Canada. The Socceroos are appearing at their sixth consecutive finals, and a seventh overall, and will hope to build on their experience in Qatar four years ago when they progressed to the knockout stage for just the second time. They faced European opposition twice in the group stage on that occasion and had mixed fortunes, beginning their campaign with a 4-1 loss to eventual runners-up France, before beating Denmark 1-0 in their final group match, which helped them to move on to a meeting with eventual winners Argentina. In contrast, Türkiye have only appeared in World Cup finals on two previous occasions, most recently in 2002 when the competition had co-hosts for the first time in Japan and South Korea, and they will hope to perform just as admirably as they did on that occasion when they reached the semi-finals and were beaten 1-0 by eventual champions Brazil

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Ivory Coast v Ecuador (Kick-off 12.00am). Coverage of the Group E match at Philadelphia Stadium in the USA. This will be the first-ever meeting between these nations, and both will be aiming to get their campaigns off to a winning start having performed admirably in qualifying for this tournament. Les Éléphants finished atop their group in the CAF region after finishing with a record of eight wins and two draws from their 10 matches, while La Tricolor finished second in the highly competitive CONMEBOL region, trailing only Argentina, even after they were deducted three points related to an infringement from a previous World Cup qualification cycle. Neither of these nations has a stellar World Cup record from previous appearances, with Ivory Coast never having progressed beyond the group stage, and not having qualified since 2014, while Ecuador have appeared at four World Cup finals, most recently four years ago, making it to the last-16 on just one occasion

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Sweden v Tunisia (Kick-off 3.00am). Coverage of the Group F match at Monterrey Stadium in Mexico. Sweden have a fairly disappointing record of qualifying for the World Cup in recent times, only appearing three times since they finished third at USA '94, and they had to rely on the UEFA play-offs to secure their spot this time around. For Tunisia this is their third consecutive appearance at a World Cup finals and their seventh overall, and they'll hope to take advantage of the new expanded format and qualify for the knockout stage for the first time. They came up short in Qatar four years ago despite claiming a credible 0-0 draw with Denmark and memorable 1-0 victory over France, with a 1-0 loss to Australia ultimately seeing the Socceroos finish above them in Group D as they progressed to the last 16 alongside eventual runners-up, Les Bleus

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 26 Live
programme.desc: Iran v New Zealand (Kick-off 2.00am). Coverage of the opening Group G fixture for both sides, which takes place at Los Angeles Stadium in Inglewood, USA. Iran have never progressed beyond the group stage of a World Cup finals, but will feel this is their best chance yet, with so many teams involved and a relatively undaunting group facing them. They also have plenty of experience on the global stage, having reached the last three finals, especially compared to their opponents. The All Whites' last appearance in the World Cup finals was back in 2010, when they held the distinction of being the only undefeated team in the tournament, although that was of little consolation as they were eliminated from their group, along with Italy, after finishing behind Paraguay and Slovakia

programme.title: Luther: Never Too Much (2024)

programme.title: Nina Simone: Live at Montreux 1976

Other highlights include Backlash Blues and Be My Husband

programme.title: Classic Albums

programme.desc: JJ Bunce offers to kill John Treadwell's elderly mother-in-law for a fee, but the suggestion is rejected out of hand - at first

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A married man captures an armed criminal who tried to rob him and his mistress, but to avoid a scandal, he contemplates whether to let him go or kill him

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A profile of the Welsh actor, who was 13 when he starred in Empire of the Sun and has since played Bruce Wayne in Christopher Nolan's Batman films and won an Oscar for The Fighter

programme.title: Classic Movies: The Story Of
programme.sub-title: The Story of The Third Man
programme.desc: A look at cinematic masterpieces, beginning with film critic Ian Nathan examining the qualities of director Carol Reed's 1949 film noir The Third Man, which starred Orson Welles and Joseph Cotten

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Two archaeologists get less than they bargained for while trying to steal a priceless treasure from a tomb. Starring Nigel Havers and Richard Johnson

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A would-be burglar is confused when his intended victim - an elderly lady - appears to befriend him. Drama, with Siobhan McKenna and Patrick Field

programme.title: The Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: A convict at a state prison work camp plans a clever escape with the help of the aging, alcoholic fellow prisoner who's in charge of making coffins and burying the camp's deceased. Edd Byrnes and Robert Keith star

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Friends, fans and fellow musicians reflect on Elton John's career, exploring his greatest hits, as well as his work on The Lion King and Billy Elliot

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A profile of the American actor, whose films include Thelma & Louise, Seven, Fight Club and 12 Monkeys, and won an Oscar for his role in Once Upon a Time in Hollywood

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.sub-title: Jodie Comer, Daniel Lismore & Nick Moran
programme.desc: Stephen Mangan and Joan Bakewell host the painting challenge. Nine contestants paint celebrity sitters Jodie Comer, Daniel Lismore and Nick Moran, before the judges decide who deserves the last spot in the semi-final

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.sub-title: The Boy Who Talked with Animals
programme.desc: A father buys his son a giant turtle, but is concerned about what the boy will do with it

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A man seeks advice from his sponging friend on how to woo his boss's daughter. Drama, starring Michael Kitchen

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: Odd behaviour has some people believing a special delivery of quick-growing mushroom spores may be an invading life form

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A very rude traveling salesman forces a truck off the road, causing a delay in emergency care for an injured young man

programme.title: Painting Birds with Jim and Nancy Moir
programme.desc: The couple are joined by art-loving children from the Kent area to looking for birds found in everyday places, from gardens and playgrounds to coastlines and parks

programme.title: André Rieu
programme.desc: A selection of performances by the Dutch violinist at historic venues around the world

programme.title: André Rieu: Live in Dublin
programme.desc: The violinist and his Johann Strauss Orchestra perform some of his best-loved music in the Irish capital city

programme.title: Classic FM Live: Classical Anthems
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Myleene Klass present a concert from the Royal Albert Hall, with performances from Freddie De Tommaso, Hayato Sumino and more

programme.title: Matisse: The Last Masterpiece
programme.desc: A look at the story of the creation of the Chapelle du Rosaire in the French commune of Vence, revealing how Henri Matisse was inspired by nurse Monique Bourgeois

programme.title: The Pilgrimage of Gilbert & George
programme.desc: A profile of the art duo who have been at the forefront of British art for over half a century, weaving together interviews with historic and rare archive to create an engrossing reflection on their journey

programme.title: Under Salt Marsh
programme.desc: Old accusations resurface as Jackie and Bull must put aside years of mistrust before the town's tensions ignite into violence. Crime drama, starring Kelly Reilly and Rafe Spall

programme.title: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms
programme.sub-title: In the Name of the Mother
programme.desc: Dunk's mettle is put to the test in the brutal trial of seven, while a flashback reveals how he found himself drawn to the promise of a new future

programme.title: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms
programme.desc: As Ashford mourns a great loss, Dunk considers his next move and decides whether to keep Egg as his squire. Peter Claffey and Dexter Sol Ansell star

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: First episode of the drama set in a lawless US town in the aftermath of Little Big Horn, where everyone's a gold digger, from hopeful prospectors to the saloon hookers. Former Montana marshal Seth Bullock relocates to a gold-mining camp known as Deadwood in Dakota Territory, looking to start a hardware business with his partner, Sol Star. Starring Ian McShane as ruthless saloon owner Al Swearengen, Timothy Olyphant as store manager Seth Bullock and Molly Parker as Alma Garrett

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Calamity Jane and Doc Cochran care for the little girl who survived the massacre, as suspicions arise that the Indians may not have been to blame. Hickok asks Bullock for protection in a high-stakes poker game, and Tom Mason is determined to exact his revenge. Starring Ian McShane, Timothy Olyphant and Nick Offerman

programme.title: The Guest Wing
programme.desc: Everyday life at Northumberland's Chillingham Castle, where the members of staff try to tactfully deal with the whims of their employer, baronet Humphry Wakefield, as the estate prepares to welcome a band of ghost-hunting tourists. Meanwhile, Carlton Towers in North Yorkshire hosts an annual dog show for Afghan hounds

programme.title: Sort Of
programme.desc: Raffo finds herself at an emotional edge, while Wolf surprises Sabi with a gift. 7ven and Mackenzie organise a party, before shutting it down

programme.title: The Guest Wing
programme.desc: Lord and Lady Fitzalan Howard try to impress American guests at Carlton Towers with a programme of traditional activities, including skinning rabbits and taking high tea. Meanwhile, Sir Humphry Wakefield entertains tourists on a wine-tasting weekend at Chillingham Castle

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor heads to Tokyo and checks out the Park Hyatt, the hotel featured in the film Lost in Translation, and goes behind the closed doors of the Japanese capital's love hotels. In the fashionable district of Meguro, Richard takes in the Claska, which was the city's first boutique hotel and has its own canine beauty salon

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: Richard E Grant travels to Venice, said to be the city with the highest concentration of luxury hotels on the planet. He discovers how a measuring error led to the building of the city's biggest swimming pool, tries out a 15,000 euro-a-night royal suite whose bed has been slept in by everyone from the Beckhams to Prince Charles and the Duchess of Cornwall, and visits a hotel that keeps its doors open even when water floods to knee height. He also finds out why breaking a window once earned Ernest Hemingway a 10 per cent discount on his room

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: Axe and Chuck fight for the loyalty of the same witness. Taylor travels to Silicon Valley to explore new business opportunities

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: Axe tracks down a critical piece of evidence that could destroy him, and the Ice Juice case comes to a head when a judge gives Connerty a difficult deadline

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: It is Easter morning 1883 and Bertha Russell receives news that her bid for a box at the Academy of Music has been rejected. The return of the period drama, starring Carrie Coon and Louisa Jacobson

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: Peggy gets welcomed back to 61st Street by almost everyone, while George informs Oscar of his decision and sends Clay to meet with union leader Mr Henderson at his steel mill

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: The opera war escalates, there is a surprise guest at Bertha's fundraiser, and Peggy offers to cover the Tuskegee story. Ada hosts a luncheon for Mr Forte, George woos Henderson and Watson mulls over a proposal

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: After receiving discouraging news about the Met, Bertha deals with rumours that are swirling around about Larry. Fearing her sister's disapproval, Ada asks Marian to cover for her as she continues to see Mr Forte. Bertha vies for position with the visiting Duke of Buckingham, and, in Tuskegee, Peggy navigates a tense dinner with Mr Fortune and Booker T Washington in Tuskegee

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: After sharing her news, Ada questions moving ahead without Agnes's support, while Peggy experiences the dangers of the South

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: George heads to Pittsburgh to deal with the potential strike, while Bertha learns that the Duke will return for the Metropolitan Opera's opening night. Bannister tries to retrieve an incriminating letter from the Russell house

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: Bertha begins to rethink her loyalty to the Metropolitan Opera, an engagement party leaves Marian full of doubts, and George strikes a deal with union leader Bill Henderson

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.sub-title: In Terms of Winning and Losing
programme.desc: The staff ponder their future at Rhijn's house as Peggy questions her own at the New York Globe. Marian confesses her true feelings to Dashiell and the opera war reaches its climax.

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: In Arizona, George faces opposition while unfurling his ambitious plan to build a train route connecting New York, Chicago and Los Angeles. Back in New York City, Bertha prepares for the Duke's imminent arrival, Peggy falls ill after an unseasonable snowstorm, and Ada hosts a gathering to support the temperance movement. Period drama, starring Carrie Coon and Morgan Spector

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: Concerned about Peggy's worsening condition, Agnes and Ada welcome her parents and their physician Dr Kirkland to 61st Street. Meanwhile, Oscar gets a professional favour from an old friend, Bertha makes her case for the duke to a sceptical George, and Gladys suffers a heartbreaking disappointment

programme.title: Task
programme.desc: After scaling back his role in the FBI and still reeling from a family tragedy, Special Agent Tom Brandis reluctantly returns to the field to head a new, relatively inexperienced team to investigate a string of robberies in Philadelphia. Following his brother's death, Robbie Prendergrast earns extra cash for his family by ripping off drug dealers - but a bad tip upends his delicate balance. Crime drama, starring Mark Ruffalo

programme.title: The Fear Index
programme.desc: A fatal plane crash causes airline stocks to plummet. Alex harbours his own concerns when he finds terrorist websites open on his computer

programme.title: Task
programme.desc: After scaling back his role in the FBI and still reeling from a family tragedy, Special Agent Tom Brandis reluctantly returns to the field to head a new, relatively inexperienced team to investigate a string of robberies in Philadelphia. Following his brother's death, Robbie Prendergrast earns extra cash for his family by ripping off drug dealers - but a bad tip upends his delicate balance. Crime drama, starring Mark Ruffalo

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Al is furious when Cy Tolliver appears in Deadwood planning to open a rival brothel, and vows to find out who helped the crafty businessman sneak into town. Meanwhile, Brom Garrett confronts the saloon owner over his dud gold claim. Starring Ian McShane, Timothy Olyphant and Molly Parker

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Alma enlists Hickok's help in an attempt to get to the bottom of her husband's death. However, there is also a plot being made against Bill's own life

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: The trial of Jack McCall continues as the jury retire to mull over the evidence they have heard. Elsewhere, the townsfolk lay the outlaw Wild Bill to rest, and Bullock decides to take the law into his own hands

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: The smallpox virus threatens the very existence of the inhabitants of Deadwood, as it engulfs the entire town and leaves no one well enough to go and get any medicine. Meanwhile, Swearengen is still after Alma's gold claim

programme.title: The Guest Wing
programme.desc: The Penningtons, owners of Muncaster Castle in the Lake District, discuss the challenges of housing three generations of their family under one roof, while elderly patriarch Patrick struggles to find new purpose following the death of his wife. At Maunsel House in Somerset, Sir Benjamin Slade's preconceptions are challenged when a gay men's choir arrives for a weekend break

programme.title: The Guest Wing
programme.desc: At Northumberland's Chillingham Castle, Sir Humphrey Wakefield prepares to hand over the estate to his apprehensive son Maximilian. Hard-working staff at Carlton Towers in North Yorkshire get ready for the arrival of 120 caravan owners for the annual rally and dinner dance

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Life in the Devon fishing port of Brixham. Spring is in the air, and the town is a hive of activity as skippers return their pleasure yachts to the water after winter in the dry dock, while the pressure is on for Brixham to look its best for the arrival of Princess Anne, who will officially open the new £20million fish market, and the start of the summer season

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Brixham hosts its annual Pirate Festival, paying tribute to Devon's colourful maritime history, while fisherman Nathan sets off in search of a lucrative catch

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Detectives Munch and Gharty investigate the case of a basketball star found shot dead, and find themselves wondering whether he really was the wholesome character he appeared. Meanwhile, Pembleton and Bayliss crack an old case and Kellerman receives good news for a change

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Two women are found strangled, and evidence shows that on the night of their death they were bought drinks by a married couple

programme.title: Hacks
programme.desc: After learning there's a Late Night vacancy, Deborah makes sure everything - and everyone - is operating at its finest for her roast. Comedy drama, starring Jean Smart, Hannah Einbinder and Carl Clemons-Hopkins

programme.title: Hacks
programme.desc: While in New York preparing for the Thanksgiving parade, Deborah receives an invite to hang with some of her favourite comedians back in Vegas. Jimmy finally lands a general meeting with Winnie Landell thanks to Kayla. Comedy drama, starring Jean Smart, Paul W Downs and Helen Hunt

programme.title: Westworld
programme.desc: Charlotte begins to see her plans realised and Bernard and Maeve fight to save the world. The Man in Black returns to the game

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.sub-title: Jewel's Boot Is Made for Walking
programme.desc: Jewel continues to come under fire for the problems with her leg, so she visits Doc Cochran bearing pictures of braces, which she hopes will ease her discomfort. Meanwhile, Trixie has an appointment with Sol, and Alma's father comes to town

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Seth Bullock puts self-doubt aside and appoints himself sheriff of Deadwood. Meanwhile, three men meet a grisly end with the deaths of the Rev Smith, Claggett and Otis Russell, the latter coming at the hands of the town's new law enforcer

programme.title: Mussolini: Son of the Century

programme.desc: With the wedding between Gladys and the duke imminent, Bertha must deal with unwanted rumours in the press, the sudden arrival of her sister Monica, and an unwilling bride.
