programme.desc: As negotiations with the duke start to disintegrate, George confronts Bertha about her vision for Gladys and its inadvertent impact on his business. Meanwhile, the Scotts meet the Kirklands in Newport, Ada encourages the staff to sign a temperance pledge, Agnes laments her slow exclusion from society, and Larry invites Jack to a meeting with a potential investor

programme.title: The Gilded Age
programme.desc: With the wedding between Gladys and the duke imminent, Bertha must deal with unwanted rumours in the press, the sudden arrival of her sister Monica, and an unwilling bride. After giving a talk in Newport, Peggy and her mother encounter the aloof Mrs Kirkland, while a grieving Ada attempts a new way to communicate with Luke, and Borden learns more about Mrs Bruce's past

programme.title: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms
programme.desc: After landing himself in the Targaryens' crosshairs, Dunk exercises his right to request a trial by combat. However, Aerion insists on a trial of seven instead, so Dunk is faced with the daunting task of finding six others to fight alongside him

programme.title: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms
programme.sub-title: In the Name of the Mother
programme.desc: Dunk's mettle is put to the test in the brutal trial of seven, while a flashback reveals how he found himself drawn to the promise of a new future

programme.title: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms
programme.desc: As Ashford mourns a great loss, Dunk considers his next move and decides whether to keep Egg as his squire. Peter Claffey and Dexter Sol Ansell star

programme.title: Task
programme.desc: After scaling back his role in the FBI and still reeling from a family tragedy, Special Agent Tom Brandis reluctantly returns to the field to head a new, relatively inexperienced team to investigate a string of robberies in Philadelphia. Following his brother's death, Robbie Prendergrast earns extra cash for his family by ripping off drug dealers - but a bad tip upends his delicate balance. Crime drama, starring Mark Ruffalo

programme.title: Ponies
programme.desc: As dark truths surface, Bea struggles to maintain her cover with Andrei, while Twila goes undercover

programme.title: Rosa Elettrica
programme.desc: A new witness protection unit agent must protect a repentant member of the Camorra criminal organisation. Italian thriller, starring Maria Chiara Giannetta and Francesco Di Napoli

programme.title: Rosa Elettrica
programme.desc: A new witness protection unit agent must protect a repentant member of the Camorra criminal organisation. Italian thriller, starring Maria Chiara Giannetta and Francesco Di Napoli

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Two women are found strangled, and evidence shows that on the night of their death they were bought drinks by a married couple

programme.title: Anora (2024)
programme.desc: A sex worker marries the son of an oligarch, but his family in Russia try to get the marriage annulled. Writer/director Sean Baker's Oscar-winning romantic comedy, starring Mikey Madison, Paul Weissman and Lindsey Normington

programme.title: Presence (2024)
programme.desc: A family become convinced they are not alone after moving into their new home in the suburbs. Steven Soderbergh's horror thriller, starring Lucy Liu, Chris Sullivan, Callina Liang and Eddy Maday

programme.title: The Cinema List: Spielberg
programme.desc: This edition of the Cinema List celebrates Steven Spielberg. From the new Disclosure Day to Jurassic Park, these blockbuster adventures are top tier

programme.title: Corpse Bride (2005)
programme.desc: Shy bridegroom Victor unintentionally gets himself hitched to a ghostly bride, who lures him down into the netherworld. But as he strives to find his way back to the land of the living, a treacherous aristocrat is scheming to marry Victor's flesh-and-blood fiancee. Tim Burton's stop-motion animated fantasy, with the voices of Johnny Depp, Helena Bonham Carter, Emily Watson, Christopher Lee and Richard E Grant

programme.title: Hook (1991)
programme.desc: Peter Pan, who has finally grown up and is now a married lawyer with a family of his own, is whisked away by Tinkerbell to Neverland, where he must learn to fly again if he is to save his children from the clutches of Captain Hook. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure, with Robin Williams, Dustin Hoffman, Julia Roberts, Bob Hoskins and Maggie Smith

programme.title: The BFG (2016)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl befriends a benevolent giant, who takes her off to a magical land, where they attempt to stop the man-eating creatures that are invading the human world. Steven Spielberg's fantasy adventure based on Roald Dahl's book, starring Mark Rylance, Ruby Barnhill and Penelope Wilton

programme.title: E.T.: The Extra-Terrestrial (1982)
programme.desc: A lonely boy befriends an alien who has been left stranded on Earth. The two develop a psychic connection as the boy helps the extraterrestrial to contact his own people and call for rescue. However, living on Earth starts to have a devastating effect on the visitor's health, and a government operative is determined to capture him for study. Steven Spielberg's sci-fi fantasy, starring Henry Thomas, Drew Barrymore, Dee Wallace and Peter Coyote

programme.title: Jaws (1975)
programme.desc: A New England seaside town's tourist season is turned into a nightmare by a giant killer shark. The local police chief's efforts to protect the public are thwarted by the greedy mayor, forcing him to join forces with a grizzled fisherman and an enthusiastic marine biologist to hunt and kill the monster predator. Steven Spielberg's blockbusting thriller, starring Roy Scheider, Richard Dreyfuss and Robert Shaw, with an Oscar-winning score by John Williams. The film also won Academy Awards for Best Sound and Best Film Editing

programme.title: Empire of the Sun (1987)
programme.desc: An English boy living in Shanghai is separated from his parents during the Japanese invasion and forced to live on the streets. The youngster is befriended by an American sailor, who helps the boy adapt to life without his family, before the pair are eventually captured and subjected to the terrors of internment. Steven Spielberg's Second World War drama based on JG Ballard's semi-autobiographical novel, starring Christian Bale, John Malkovich and Nigel Havers

programme.title: Catch Me If You Can (2002)
programme.desc: The story of Frank Abagnale Jr, who found notoriety as a teenager conning and charming his way across America. Posing as a pilot, a doctor and a lawyer, Frank was able to fund an extravagant lifestyle while outwitting the FBI at every turn. Fact-based drama, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Tom Hanks, Christopher Walken, Martin Sheen and James Brolin

programme.title: The Post (2017)
programme.desc: In 1971, an American military analyst uncovers the depths of the US government's deceptions about the futility of the Vietnam War, and takes action by copying top-secret documents that would become the Pentagon Papers. Washington Post owner Kay Graham and her editor Ben Bradlee discover The New York Times is preparing an explosive expose on those papers and, determined to compete, set out to publish them first. Steven Spielberg's fact-based drama, starring Meryl Streep, Tom Hanks, Sarah Paulson and Bob Odenkirk

programme.title: Erin Brockovich (2000)
programme.desc: A single mother desperate for work pleads her way into a routine administrative job at a law firm. It is not long before she stumbles across mislaid documents about a case brought against a corporation for poisoning the residents of a small town by contaminating their water supply. Realising something is amiss, she begins her own investigation. Fact-based drama, with Julia Roberts in an Oscar-winning role, alongside Albert Finney and Aaron Eckhart

programme.title: Eddington (2025)
programme.desc: A stand-off between a small-town sheriff and mayor sparks a powder keg as neighbours are pitted against each other in Eddington, New Mexico. Modern-day Western, starring Joaquin Phoenix, Emma Stone and Pedro Pascal

programme.title: Black Bag (2025)
programme.desc: When a spy is suspected of treason, her husband faces the ultimate test - loyalty to his marriage or his country. Director Steven Soderbergh's thriller, starring Michael Fassbender, Cate Blanchett and Rege-Jean Page

programme.title: The Cinema List: Spielberg
programme.desc: This edition of the Cinema List celebrates Steven Spielberg. From the new Disclosure Day to Jurassic Park, these blockbuster adventures are top tier

programme.title: Presence (2024)
programme.desc: A family become convinced they are not alone after moving into their new home in the suburbs. Steven Soderbergh's horror thriller, starring Lucy Liu, Chris Sullivan, Callina Liang and Eddy Maday

programme.title: Through a Glass Darkly (1961)
programme.desc: A former mental institution inmate returns to her family, but faces an uphill struggle to readjust to life in the outside world. Ingmar Bergman's drama, starring Harriet Andersson and Max von Sydow. In Swedish

programme.title: Fanny and Alexander (1982)
programme.desc: Ingmar Bergman's Oscar-winning semi-autobiographical drama follows the lives of two children from a theatrical family in turn of the century Sweden. Their carefree lives are thrown into upheaval by the death of their father and their mother's subsequent remarriage to a puritanical minister. But refuge from their joyless new home comes in the shape of their grandmother and her lover. Starring Pernilla Allwin, Bertil Guve, Gunn Wallgren and Lena Olin

programme.title: Cries and Whispers (1972)
programme.desc: Agnes, a woman dying of cancer, is visited in her remote home by her two sisters. Far from offering comfort, their arrival brings long-buried family conflicts bubbling to the surface, with tensions rising as Agnes' health deteriorates. Ingmar Bergman's drama, starring Harriet Andersson, Liv Ullmann and Ingrid Thulin. In Swedish

programme.title: The Post (2017)
programme.desc: In 1971, an American military analyst uncovers the depths of the US government's deceptions about the futility of the Vietnam War, and takes action by copying top-secret documents that would become the Pentagon Papers. Washington Post owner Kay Graham and her editor Ben Bradlee discover The New York Times is preparing an explosive expose on those papers and, determined to compete, set out to publish them first. Steven Spielberg's fact-based drama, starring Meryl Streep, Tom Hanks, Sarah Paulson and Bob Odenkirk

programme.title: The Room Next Door (2024)
programme.desc: Former war correspondent Martha is resigned to the grip of inoperable stage three cervical cancer. She plans to spend her final weeks in a rented property near Woodstock with a euthanasia pill obtained illegally on the dark web. While she awkwardly articulates her desire to stay in control, her friend Ingrid seeks comfort in the company of mutual old flame Damian. Pedro Almodovar's drama, his first feature shot in the English language, starring Tilda Swinton, Julianne Moore, John Turturro and Alessandro Nivola

programme.title: Erin Brockovich (2000)
programme.desc: A single mother desperate for work pleads her way into a routine administrative job at a law firm. programme.desc: A sex worker marries the son of an oligarch, but his family in Russia try to get the marriage annulled. Writer/director Sean Baker's Oscar-winning romantic comedy, starring Mikey Madison, Paul Weissman and Lindsey Normington

programme.title: Caught Stealing (2025)
programme.desc: Former baseball player Henry 'Hank' Thompson foolishly agrees to cat-sit for British neighbour Russ, and walks unwittingly into the middle of a turf war between warring factions. Director Darren Aronofsky's comedy thriller, starring Austin Butler, Zoe Kravitz and Matt Smith

programme.title: Se7en (1995)
programme.desc: An old-school detective and his headstrong new partner discover a connection between two unusually horrific murders, and realise they are on the trail of a serial killer who deems his victims to be each guilty of one of the seven deadly sins. However, the longer the cops pursue the murderer, the ever more twisted and disturbing the slayings become. David Fincher's thriller, starring Brad Pitt, Morgan Freeman, Gwyneth Paltrow and Kevin Spacey

programme.title: Black Bag (2025)
programme.desc: When a spy is suspected of treason, her husband faces the ultimate test - loyalty to his marriage or his country. Director Steven Soderbergh's thriller, starring Michael Fassbender, Cate Blanchett and Rege-Jean Page

programme.title: Him
programme.desc: Cameron Cade is predicted to be the next great quarterback of American football. During a night-time training session, he suffers a traumatic brain injury. Cameron agrees to attend a week-long boot camp run by retiring star quarterback Isaiah White at his secluded desert compound. However, Cade's life soon turns into a nightmare. Supernatural horror, starring Marlon Wayans and Tyriq Withers

programme.title: The Internship (2026)
programme.desc: A CIA-trained assassin recruits other graduates from her secret childhood programme, the Internship, to violently destroy the organisation. Thriller, starring Lizzy Greene, Megan Boone and Sky Katz

programme.title: Tafiti - Across The Desert (2025)
programme.desc: A young meerkat's grandpa is bitten by a snake, so he sets off to find a magical flower for the cure. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Cosima Henman, Steve Hudson and Kathleen Renish

programme.title: No Ordinary Heist (2026)
programme.desc: Two feuding bank employees are forced to execute Ireland's biggest heist after criminals kidnap their families and threaten to kill them. Fact-based thriller, starring Eddie Marsan and Michelle Fairley

programme.title: They Will Kill You (2026)
programme.desc: A woman takes a job as a housekeeper in a New York City high-rise, unaware of the building's history of disappearances. She soon realises the community is shrouded in mystery. Comedy horror, starring Zazie Beetz, Myha'la, Paterson Joseph and Tom Felton

programme.title: Fuze (2026)
programme.desc: An unexploded Second World War bomb is discovered on a busy construction site in the centre of London. Chaos ensues as the military and police begin a mass evacuation. Drama, starring Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Theo James, Gugu Mbatha-Raw and Sam Worthington

programme.desc: Tony Kiritsis is convinced mortgage broker ML Hall cheated him out of profits from a land development deal and he vows revenge. Tony walks into a branch of the Meridian Mortgage company for a meeting with ML, only to find his target is on holiday and the appointment will be with ML's son Richard instead. Regardless, Tony reveals a shotgun and holds Richard hostage for his father's supposed crimes. Director Gus Van Sant's thriller, starring Bill Skarsgård, Al Pacino and Dacre Montgomery

programme.title: Sisu: Road to Revenge (2025)
programme.desc: During the Second World War, Finland reluctantly cedes territory to the invading Red Army. Former Army commando Aatami Korpi is among the affected and returns to his family plot to deconstruct his timber-framed home and relocate the wooden beams on a truck across the new border separating the Soviet Union and Finland. The legend of the 'the man who refuses to die' remains a thorn in the side of the Soviet army, and a nameless KGB officer agrees to liberate high-ranking brute Igor Draganov from a Siberian prison in exchange for killing Aatami. Finnish action sequel, starring Jorma Tommila, Stephen Lang and Richard Brake

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile of serial killer Robert Lee Yates, who narrowly avoided the death penalty for killing at least 13 sex workers during a horrific reign of terror

programme.sub-title: Carlson: The Stranger in the Night
programme.desc: Documentary lifting the lid on police investigations into some of the most incredible murder cases in criminal history

programme.desc: In the months leading up to Rex Heuermann's guilty plea to seven murders - and his admission to an eighth - his wife and daughter reckon with the devastating truth of his confession, revealed to them in private before it became public

programme.title: Road Wars
programme.desc: The Proactive Squad from Thames Valley Police uses unmarked cars fitted with on-board video cameras to give an insight into vehicle crime and how it can be prevented, featuring footage of dangerous drivers on the overcrowded roads

programme.desc: Police target a gang of muggers terrorising a city centre, a couple's wildlife camera picks up a lowlife prowler in their back garden and a thief is hoisted in the air by an angry car owner with a forklift truck. Presented by Dominic Littlewood

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A sinister criminal posing as a police officer is chased by the real police. A cycle thief picks the wrong bike to pedal off with, while brave staff at a jewellers turn the tables on a gang of robbers. Presented by Dominic Littlewood

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood investigates the innovative new ways the police and members of the public are catching criminals in the act. He begins with the owners of a jewellery shop who purposely confused armed thieves in the hope they would flee, and there is a look at a police 'capture house' operation that is designed to snare burglars

programme.title: Send in the Dogs
programme.desc: Sgt Midgley tries to qualify as a dog handler after 24 years on the force but 14-month-old springer spaniel Ruby puts both their futures in jeopardy. The Greater Manchester Police dog squad remembers a close call with football fans during the 2008 Uefa Cup Final, and the 55-strong team deals with a siege involving a man with a knife, who is threatening to kill his family. Narrated by Ken Stott

programme.desc: A lorry breakdown causes rush-hour traffic to descend into chaos, and an emergency response team is dispatched to deal with collapsed power lines that have closed the A1

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: Witnesses and the relatives of victims reflect on the murder of 16 children and a teacher at Dunblane Primary School in March 1996, when unemployed former shopkeeper Thomas Hamilton entered the building armed with four handguns

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.sub-title: Rhys Jones: Death of an Innocent
programme.desc: A documentary on the death of Rhys Jones, an 11-year-old who was shot and killed while walking home from football practice in Croxteth, Liverpool, in August 2007. The assailant was 18-year-old Sean Mercer, who was firing at rival gang members and hit the child in the process. An investigation to bring Mercer and his gang to justice created tension in the area and caused untold pain to the family, but the perpetrator was eventually sentenced to 22 years behind bars and six other gang members were also jailed

programme.title: WWII's Most Daring Raids
programme.desc: The efforts of Norwegian resistance fighters to return to their invaded homeland to carry out a daring mission to raid and destroy a hydroelectric plant where the Germans were stockpiling a chemical needed for nuclear weapons research

programme.title: WWII's Most Daring Raids
programme.desc: Recounting how on the outskirts of Bouerat Harbor in Libya, a small group of British special forces made plans to destroy a crucial German fuel depot, regarded as a lifeline for Nazi forces

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs around the country to learn more about mankind's Stone Age ancestors, and travels to Jersey to meet a team hoping to shed new light on the Neanderthals. In the Preseli Hills in Pembrokeshire, she learns about an investigation into the mystery surrounding Stonehenge, and hears stories of cannibalism and the ritual use of human skulls during the Ice Age

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores archaeological digs at sites dating back to the Viking age, offering an insight into how the seafaring warriors from Scandinavia lived across Britain. In the Outer Hebrides, she examines signs of the earliest Norse settlers, before inspecting a collection of silver and gold pieces discovered in York. There is also a look at some of the most treasured Viking finds, including 12th-century ivory chess figures the Lewis Chessmen

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts investigate reports of bird-like figures that practised advanced medical science in ancient times, considering whether or not they could have been extra-terrestrial

programme.title: Rome: Rise and Fall of an Empire
programme.desc: The rule of Marcus Aurelius, who was forced to leave Rome and undertake a series of military campaigns to save the Empire from the German tribes that threatened its future

programme.title: Rome: Rise and Fall of an Empire
programme.desc: How Emperor Decius seized power from his predecessor and attempted to stamp out the newly introduced Christian religion - until Rome was struck by plague and a Goth invasion

programme.title: Colosseum
programme.desc: All Roman rulers put great value on spectacles and games, but only one, the son of Marcus Aurelius, steps out of the Imperial Box and onto the sands of the arena. As Emperor Commodus makes enemies amongst the ruling Senate, a deadly game of cat and mouse ensues

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: Former detectives, victims' relatives and survivors reflect on the Real IRA car bombing that killed 29 people in Omagh, Northern Ireland, in 1998

programme.title: Colosseum
programme.desc: Drama-documentary charting the rise and fall of the Roman Empire through the Colosseum, one of the most exhilarating and brutal arenas in the history of humanity. Here, the mighty Emperor Trajan throws his great games of AD 107, and the crowd is treated to a rare spectacle - female gladiators

programme.title: Killer Kings
programme.desc: Emperor Nero, the Antichrist who killed Christians for fun. Nero was 17 when he ascended to power in AD 54 following the death of his step-father, Claudius. Fourteen chaotic, blood-spattered years later it was all over, Nero dying - perhaps by his own hand - at the climax of a rebellion against his rule

programme.title: Nazi Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The theories surrounding the death of Hermann Goering, who managed to cheat the hangman by taking a cyanide capsule on the day before his scheduled death

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts investigate reports of bird-like figures that practised advanced medical science in ancient times, considering whether or not they could have been extra-terrestrial

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: Former detectives, victims' relatives and survivors reflect on the Real IRA car bombing that killed 29 people in Omagh, Northern Ireland, in 1998

programme.title: The Secret Nazi Expeditions
programme.desc: As Hitler rose to power, Heinrich Himmler and the SS were convinced the legendary continent Atlantis existed. From an ancient city to rock carvings in Sweden, the Ahnenerbe - Nazi Germany's secret team of elite scholars and archaeologists - searched for its whereabouts

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The crucial role ships have played in international conflict over the past 500 years. This programme looks at early metal warships, and examines HMS Rattler's screw propeller in 1845 would lead to the revolutionary HMS Dreadnought in the 20th century

programme.title: Bone Detectives: Britain's Buried Secrets
programme.desc: Tori Herridge and a team of scientists attempt to piece together the lives behind discovered bones, and find out what happened to these people and how they died. In the first edition, five bodies dating from the Bronze Age are found in a deep pit on the Isle of Thanet in Kent. One of them is an old woman, who has been manipulated into a position where she is pointing at something. But at what? And why?

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: River detectorist Beau Ouimette and presenter and keen swimmer Rick Edwards go in search of evidence of the clashing Scottish clans embroiled in the civil war that raged across the British Isles in the 17th century. Fights erupted during this period between the Royalists supporting the King and the Covenanters wanting more power for church and state. Starting on the River Lochy, situated in the shadow of Ben Nevis, the pair hunt for evidence of the 1645 Battle of Inverlochy - a pivotal fight that saw the Royalist army launch a surprise attack against the ill-prepared Covenanters

programme.title: Ancient Aliens

programme.desc: Alice Roberts and Matt Williams explore finds from archaeological digs across the north of Britain, and begin at the Ness of Brodgar in the Heart of Neolithic Orkney World Heritage Site, where they view a 5,000-year-old temple. The presenters witness the unearthing of a Roman altar in Maryport, Cumbria, examine discoveries from Flixton in North Yorkshire that are around 11,000 years old, and travel to a cemetery in Ardnamurchan in the Highlands. Plus, a Tudor-era feasting hall, and a hoard of Pictish treasures

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts and Matt Williams visit archaeological digs across the country, beginning with a look at the work being carried out in the west of Britain. The presenters visit Salisbury Museum, finding out about developments at sites including the enormous lost Welsh city of Trellech and Kent's Cavern, a prehistoric cave where Britain's earliest human remains have been found

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores exciting archaeological finds across the east of Britain. A team unearths a mass grave, divers search the Thames for clues to a 17th-century tragedy and a metal detectorist makes the find of a lifetime. Presented by Alice Roberts

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores archaeological finds across the North of Britain. A team discovers clues to Scotland's first kingdoms, metal detectorists unearth a hoard of Viking treasure and a new housing development reveals a graveyard of Iron Age warriors

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs around the UK, starting with the west of Britain. She discovers lost First World War training trenches on Salisbury Plain, visits Britain's first 'double henge' discovered just down the road from Stonehenge, dives into the English Channel to explore an 18th-century East India company ship packed with silver, and unearths luxury foreign goods discovered at Tintagel, the legendary childhood home of King Arthur

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: A look at the tactics employed to pass strategically important ships under the nose of the enemy, and the use of intelligence to carry out naval attacks

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain

programme.desc: From soaring skyscrapers such as One World Trade Center to impenetrable vaults like Fort Knox, humanity's strongest structures were engineered to defy disaster. However, in a world without people, even the mightiest would begin to crack and collapse in time. This episode monitors their likely decay and destruction

programme.desc: An examination of seemingly impossible things, such as a UFO apparently tracking an American fighter plane and a strange creature recorded undulating in waters off southeastern North Carolina. Plus, strange howling sounds recorded at a truckstop along Highway 97

programme.sub-title: Sucked Up by a Tornado, Elevators Gone Wrong, Motorcycle v Flood & the Indian Ocean Tsunami of 200
programme.desc: Tony Harris breaks down the most shocking moments ever captured on camera. Plus, deadly mudslide disasters and killer whales, known as orca, attacking a boat

programme.title: Ancient Aliens

programme.title: Digging for Britain

In the Outer Hebrides, she examines signs of the earliest Norse settlers, before inspecting a collection of silver and gold pieces discovered in York. There is also a look at some of the most treasured Viking finds, including 12th-century ivory chess figures the Lewis Chessmen

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs around the country to discover what life was like during the Bronze and Iron ages before the Romans invaded. programme.title: Digging for Britain

programme.title: Hazardous History with Henry Winkler

programme.title: Hazardous History with Henry Winkler

programme.title: Hazardous History with Henry Winkler

programme.desc: The presenter sets out to uncover the mysteries of King Tutankhamun. Along the way, he encounters the enigmatic 'Alien King' Akhenaten and his wife, Nefertiti. Sailing down the Nile, he investigates how the Ancient Egyptians transported enormous stone blocks for their architectural marvels, including the 700-ton Colossi of Memnon. His quest then leads him into the Valley of the Kings, where he discovers the resting places of Akhenaten and King Tut's successor, Horemheb - before finally coming face to face with the mummy of the Boy King himself

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough employs the latest technologies to explore the violence, rivalries and deadly weaponry existing within the world of bugs. This first episode examines the survival tactics of its terrifying residents including killer ants, trap-setting spiders and beetles with the ability to shoot boiling chemicals at their enemies

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster continues his bug-eyed view of the world of creepy-crawlies, revealing how predators defuse the defences of their prey. Highlights include the cockroach wasp, busy preparing a tasty - and very live - treat for its young, the whirligig beetle, which employs a water-based radar system, and the jumping portia spider, which feeds on other arachnids

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A further selection of creepy-crawly close-ups, focusing on courtship in the bug world. Highlights include scorpions shaking their booty, the perfume-wearing nasonia wasp and the tropical cricket, for whom romance goes out of the window as it uses bribery to seal the deal with would-be partners. One potential partner to steer well clear of, however, is the praying mantis, the female of which is notorious for eating the male just after - or even during - mating. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A close-up view of sex, bug-style, as David Attenborough talks viewers through the different ways in which creepy-crawlies reproduce. Size matters for the minuscule male orb spider, creepily sneaking up on its intended and trying to mate without her noticing, while there's no rest for the lothario-like butterfly, which has plenty of notches on its proverbial bedpost. However, the harvestman spider has no use for sex at all, and reproduces by cloning itself

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough shifts the focus to bugs that prefer co-operation rather than conflict. They include burrowing cockroaches, the suitably named social spiders - which share a 30-metre web - the queen bee and her happy hive of workers and green ants pulling together to build a nest

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his close-up examination of the creepy-crawly world, shifting focus to the bugs' ultimate invention - the colony. Key residents of these minuscule mega-cities include honey bees, termites, leafcutter ants - who have a knack for farming - and their Argentine cousins, members of a community so vast it spans an entire continent

programme.title: David Attenborough's Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist examines the world of flight, travelling across the globe to chart the evolutionary story of those creatures who seemingly defy the rules of gravity by taking to the air. Initially focusing on insects, Attenborough meets a descendant of the dragonfly, before examining the Atlas beetle, which tends to restrict flight to times when it needs a mate or a fresh supply of food. More aesthetically pleasing is a group of beautiful tropical butterflies, followed by an in-depth study into the blow fly - the jet fighter of insects

programme.title: David Attenborough's Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist visits the jungles of Borneo to meet creatures that like to glide through life - the harlequin tree frog and the little-known colugo - often referred to, incorrectly, as a flying lemur. He also has a CGI-assisted encounter with the first true vertebrate flyer, the pterosaur, receives a flying lesson from a small flock of swans at Loch Lomond in Scotland and encounters the planet's largest species of bat

programme.title: David Attenborough's Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist turns his attention to creatures that dominate the skies today. He begins with the vast bird family, watching a group of vultures as they go scavenging and some peregrine falcons dive bombing for their prey. He then moves on to the floating forests of Ecuador to find out what makes the humble hummingbird tick, before ending his journey at the Gomantong bat caves of Borneo - a place he first filmed in 1972 - witnessing a mass exodus of the creatures

programme.desc: A look at the complex relationships between lobsters, scallops and crabs and how changing temperatures are bringing invasive species to the ocean

programme.title: The Wild Wild East
programme.desc: A visit to Saint-Pierre and Miquelon, one of the last remaining overseas territory of France, where semi-feral horses and ponies run wild

programme.title: The Wild Wild East
programme.desc: Many non-native species have staked a claim along the coast of Nova Scotia, but at White Point Beach Resort in Liverpool, rabbits have hopped in for an extended holiday

programme.title: The Wild Wild East
programme.desc: A single year, season by season, in the lives of the wildest animals of Canada's east coast

programme.title: Waterworld Africa
programme.desc: The secrets of the mighty Chobe River, which snakes through the African wilderness. An oasis during dry season, its vast flood plains are a magnet for wildlife

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Eighty-eight capuchin monkeys rescued from a research laboratory in Chile wake up to their first morning at the Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre in Dorset. Young chimp Rodders has trouble with a sticky melon, and the park's naughtiest resident, Seamus, comes face to face with alpha male Butch

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Wildlife vet John Lewis is given a run for his money as he tries to sedate two excitable chimps. The recently rescued male capuchins get used to life together, and orang-utan Kai proves too energetic for her companions

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Beni faces a day of reckoning, Alba is back in isolation, Pujon reclaims his role as gang leader and a team head out to rescue an infant orangutan

programme.title: Arctic Secrets
programme.desc: The dawn of the Arctic spring, when the sun rises above the horizon for the first time in months, bringing the region into life

programme.title: Gangs of Lemur Island
programme.desc: In Madagascar's remote Berenty Reserve, battle lines are drawn as two ring-tailed lemur gangs face the mounting challenges of the dry season. In the Ruins Gang, Crystal's reign of fear makes life hard for some of her subjects, while her rival gang queen Kati of the Museum Gang has found a good food supply for her crew

programme.title: Africa's Hunters
programme.desc: Documentary following a leopard that has dominated two miles of territory in the Luangwa Valley for 10 years, but now finds age taking its toll

programme.title: Secrets of Wild Australia

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: The Grimms go looking for Wesen at a local boxing club after the death of an ex-fighter. Chavez tries to recruit Trubel to a secret society, and Elizabeth joins Monroe and Rosalee in a search for a cure for Nick

programme.title: Britannia
programme.desc: Aulus struggles to remain in control now that Hemple is on the scene. The Druids are splintered by the betrayal of the Chosen One

programme.title: Britannia
programme.desc: Despite growing concerns from his inner circle, Aulus reluctantly follows Hemple's guidance to return to the Underworld and find a new link to Cait

programme.title: Britannia
programme.desc: Antedia and Lucius bond over their mutual wish for revenge. Lucius is recognised as a deserter by a Roman Escort after a tip-off from prisoners Rufus and Felix

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding and a team of investigators visit Speke Hall, a Tudor manor in Liverpool where a ghostly white lady is said to roam the hallways

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Part one of two. Yvette Fielding and guest medium Brian Shepherd go ghost-hunting in the tunnels of Chislehurst Caves in Kent, a former mine that was used as an ammunition depot in World War One and an air raid shelter during World War Two

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding is joined by guest medium Barrie John as the team spend a night in the horrifying Pembrey Woods, haunted by tales of phantoms

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: A salvage operation goes wrong, leaving the team members trapped on a Goa'uld mothership and when they accidentally activate the self-destruct mechanism, they have only 17 minutes to escape. Sci-fi drama, starring Richard Dean Anderson

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: O'Neill contracts a deadly virus from a woman who has been found frozen in ice near an Antarctic research station for thousands of years - and only a symbiote from the Tok'ra can save his life. Sci-fi drama, starring Richard Dean Anderson and Amanda Tapping

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Carter, Teal'c and Jonas are sent to a seemingly sleepy seaside town on the Oregon coast to search for a scientist. They discover the community is hiding a sinister secret and eventually find an alien ship hidden in one of the buildings. Sci-fi drama, starring Richard Dean Anderson

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Colonel O'Neill is captured by the Goa'uld and tortured to the brink of death by Ba'al the System Lord - but help arrives in the form of old friend Daniel Jackson. Sci-fi drama, starring Richard Dean Anderson and Michael Shanks

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Jonas convinces Pentagon officials that a sinister plot is afoot to destroy the people of his home planet. Sci-fi drama, starring Richard Dean Anderson

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: McKay is rescued from a Wraith ship with security officer Laura Cadman. However, an unforeseen technical malfunction causes their minds to become merged, and they are condemned to co-exist in the same body. David Hewlett and Jaime Ray Newman star

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team stumbles upon a planet whose inhabitants have a far superior technological know-how, and remain virtually untouched by the Wraith. As they head back to Atlantis, however, their craft is fired upon by a gang of criminals - and they are forced to make an emergency landing on a prison island. Joe Flanigan stars

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: McKay discovers a potent source of energy based on ancient technology, which he believes can be harnessed and put to profitable use. However, as he investigates the possibility further, he inadvertently places the rest of the team at risk

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Beckett creates a potion designed to convert Wraiths back to human form. The team is surprised to encounter a mysterious girl in the early stages of the experiment, and Sheppard's life is inadvertently put at risk

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The team is mystified when Sheppard starts displaying enhanced physical strength and reflexes. Beckett runs tests on his DNA, which indicate he has been infected by his contact with Ellia - and is slowly transforming into a Wraith. Meanwhile, Weir asks Caldwell to take over the officer's duties. Joe Flanigan stars

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Beth wakes up in a hospital, but it soon becomes apparent those working there may not have her best interests at heart. She starts to work off her debt to the staff, but plots her escape with another worker called Noah - which is no mean feat as the area is surrounded by walkers

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Abraham and some of the group head to Washington, DC, but face danger along the way when their transport fails, and they must find safety. However, not everyone in the team is on board with the new plan

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Nick battles a creature made of living clay. Rosalee is troubled to discover many Wesen are angered by her engagement, and Trubel considers leaving town for her friends' sake. Meanwhile, the team makes a breakthrough regarding Nick's powers

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Nick and Hank are called to the scene of a crime where the suspect swears she was driven to madness by a talking wolf. Adalind continues searching for her daughter in Austria. Juliette learns she is the key to restoring Nick's powers, while Trubel is warned of a threat to her mentor's life

programme.title: Primeval
programme.desc: The team's new anomaly detector fails on its first outing when a paintballer is attacked at an adventure park. Refusing to accept the machine does not work, Cutter starts to think of a conventional explanation, while Connor disgraces himself by accidentally shooting the park's mascot. When the real culprit is revealed, the professor is forced to consider the possibility of human involvement as well as an anomaly. Guest starring Casualty's Gillian Kearney

programme.title: Britannia
programme.desc: Cait, Divis, Antedia, Ania and Phelan strike a deal with Hella, who is paid to reveal where Lucius can be found. Meanwhile, Amena tries to prove her worth to Hemple

programme.title: Britannia
programme.desc: Cait and Divis harness the power of Big Pebble in their search for the traitor. At the Villa, Hemple plans another feast to satisfy Lokka's hunger

programme.title: Britannia
programme.desc: Cait and her band continue their quest to reach Lucius because of his knowledge of the Spear. Hemple is furious that Veran seems to have got the better of her

programme.title: Britannia
programme.desc: Still searching for Lucius and his knowledge of The Spear, Cait takes the fight to the redoubt, and to all those who are waiting for her

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding and the team visit Inveraray Jail in Argyll and Bute, a 19th-century courthouse and prison reputedly haunted by the lost souls of convicts, vagrants and madmen

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding and Derek Acorah visit Pleasley Vale Mills in Bolsover, alleged scene of many paranormal events

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding and Derek Accorah carry out investigations in a Welsh castle and discover some unwanted residents

programme.title: Star Trek: Enterprise
programme.desc: The ship becomes stranded in a Romulan minefield, forcing Reed to attempt a risky operation. Fifth sci-fi series in the Star Trek saga, starring Scott Bakula and Dominic Keating

programme.title: Star Trek: Voyager
programme.desc: Fifteen years after Voyager crashed into a barren planet, survivors Chakotay and Kim steal a craft and try to change the fate of their long-dead crew members. Sci-fi drama, starring Garrett Wang and Robert Beltran

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: The crew stumbles upon a dilapidated ship and while exploring the vessel finds an ancient civilisation, frozen in suspended animation. It emerges that the members of the race are living in a virtual world - and their alternative reality is also inhabited by the evil Wraith

programme.title: Primeval
programme.desc: When an 11-year-old girl wanders through an anomaly into a desert, ARC traitor Oliver Leek is first on the scene with a heavily armed team of mercenaries at his disposal - but they soon fall victim to deadly scorpions lying below the sand. Cutter and Stephen eventually make their way through and manage to locate the girl, only to get stranded when the portal closes behind them. Douglas Henshall stars

programme.title: Primeval
programme.desc: Cutter sets out to unmask the ARC traitor, only for his plans to be interrupted when a giant mammoth appears on a busy motorway in broad daylight. The team rushes to the site, and eventually manages to trap the beast in the back of a lorry - but suspiciously, Stephen is nowhere to be found. Douglas Henshall stars, with an appearance by Ramon Tikaram

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: The Goa'uld capture the team members while at an archaeological dig on an alien world, leaving their fate in the hands of two research scientists, Coombs and Felger. Sci-fi drama, guest starring Enterprise's John Billingsley, with Richard Dean Anderson

programme.title: Star Trek: Voyager
programme.desc: Fifteen years after Voyager crashed into a barren planet, survivors Chakotay and Kim steal a craft and try to change the fate of their long-dead crew members. Sci-fi drama, starring Garrett Wang and Robert Beltran

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Nick and Hank spend Christmas pursuing three teenage Wesen who have an uncontrollable urge to break into houses and wreak havoc during the 12 days of the festive season

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Daryl and Carol go in search of Beth and her kidnappers as they follow the car with the white cross, taking shelter in a familiar building in Atlanta when their transport fails - and facing ever-present danger from walkers

programme.title: Westworld
programme.desc: Seven years after the demise of Rehoboam, events are set in motion that reunite allies and enemies. Starring Thandiwe Newton and Ed Harris

programme.title: Primeval
programme.desc: An anomaly opens underwater, dragging a teenager into a flooded drain leading to a canal. As the team launches a frantic search, Jenny narrowly escapes with her life when her boat is attacked by a shark - although the youngster is not so lucky. The team is convinced he was killed by the big fish, but Cutter is not so sure, believing there is a different creature out there. Sci-fi drama, starring Douglas Henshall, Lucy Brown and Hannah Spearritt

programme.title: The Hangover Part III (2013)
programme.desc: Drinking buddies Phil and Stu try to help their troubled friend Alan get his life in order, only to stumble into another bizarre misadventure. The Mob have taken Alan's brother hostage and to free him, they have to track down manic gangster Mr Chow, who has escaped from a Thai prison. Comedy sequel, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms, Zach Galifianakis and John Goodman

programme.title: The Hangover (2009)
programme.desc: Three men on a stag weekend in Las Vegas wake up after a night of heavy drinking and find they have not only misplaced the groom, but somehow gained a tiger and a baby. Unable to remember the details of the night before, they set about piecing the clues together in the hope of finding their missing friend. Comedy, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms and Zach Galifianakis

programme.title: The Hangover Part II (2011)
programme.desc: Phil, Stu and Alan reunite in Bangkok for another stag weekend, but having learned the lessons of their previous misadventures, resolve to keep this one quiet and alcohol-free. Things inevitably do not go according to plan, and they must once again piece together the events of a wild night they cannot remember. Comedy sequel, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms and Zach Galifianakis

Comedy, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms and Zach Galifianakis

programme.title: The Naked Gun: From the Files of Police Squad! (1988)
programme.desc: While investigating the shooting of a colleague, disaster-prone police lieutenant Frank Drebin uncovers a plot to assassinate the Queen during her visit to Los Angeles. However, after he is sacked from the force by the mayor, the cop has to operate beneath the radar to get to the bottom of the conspiracy. Comedy, with Leslie Nielsen, Priscilla Presley and Ricardo Montalban

programme.title: The Hangover Part II (2011)
programme.desc: Phil, Stu and Alan reunite in Bangkok for another stag weekend, but having learned the lessons of their previous misadventures, resolve to keep this one quiet and alcohol-free. Things inevitably do not go according to plan, and they must once again piece together the events of a wild night they cannot remember. Comedy sequel, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms and Zach Galifianakis

programme.title: The Hangover Part III (2013)
programme.desc: Drinking buddies Phil and Stu try to help their troubled friend Alan get his life in order, only to stumble into another bizarre misadventure. The Mob have taken Alan's brother hostage and to free him, they have to track down manic gangster Mr Chow, who has escaped from a Thai prison. Comedy sequel, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms, Zach Galifianakis and John Goodman

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames investigate the case of a heart surgeon murdered during his son's bar mitzvah. Although they initially suspect someone with Mob connections, the detectives later discover another man's obsession puts him in the frame for the killing. Drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe

programme.title: Road Wars
programme.desc: Documentary following the Thames Valley Police as they use unmarked cars fitted with on-board video cameras, offering an insight into vehicle crime and how it can be prevented. The programme also features shocking footage of dangerous drivers

programme.title: Border Security: America's Front Line
programme.desc: Cameras follow US border protection agencies as they man the front lines of national security

programme.title: Transatlantic Sessions - Togha agus Rogha
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis and Muireann Nic Amhlaoibh present highlights from two decades of Transatlantic Sessions. This episode features musical stars from Ireland, the UK and the US, including James Taylor, Phil Cunningham and Kathy Mattea. Ricky Skaggs and Sharon White sing Mansion on the Hill, while Dan Tyminski performs the popular traditional bluegrass song The Boy Who Wouldn't Hoe Corn. Plus, the much-missed Nanci Griffith performs a cover of Bob Dylan's Boots of Spanish Leather

programme.title: Carna an Cheoil agus na nAmhrán
programme.desc: For over a century, music collectors have flocked to Carna in Connemara, drawn by its rich Gaelic traditions. Here, songs, music, dance, and storytelling have been preserved, offering a rare glimpse into Ireland's ancient cultural roots. The echoes of those early collectors still linger in Carna today, and their recordings continue to shape its cultural identity. This programme examines Carna's vibrant heritage, exploring the treasures passed down through generations and meeting the new wave of locals proudly keeping their native culture alive today

programme.title: Nuacht TG4
programme.desc: The day's main news stories from a regional, national and international perspective

programme.title: Abair Amhrán
programme.desc: Featuring music by Brian Kennedy, Deirdre Ní Chinnéide, Liam Ó Maonlaí and Cór Ban Chúil Aodh and Maigh Seola

programme.title: Cúramóirí
programme.desc: Documentary delving into the heart of daily Irish life, illustrating the crucial work and life saving care provided by family carers, professional carers and dedicated organisations to those who need it the most. In this episode, Mairead gives a portrait of life as a family carer and Janie Connolly shares her story of the impact of childhood cancer. Plus, the Ionad Lae in Gaoth Dobhair offers an insight into the services they provide

programme.title: Hector Caribbean
programme.desc: Hector Ó hEochagáin samples the tasty delicacies of Puerto Rico and explores the island's vibrant street art scene in the bustling capital of San Juan. He scores a slam dunk with a visit to a local basketball court and visits a pineapple farm, the key ingredient of the Caribbean's signature cocktail, the piña colada. Hector also gets to grips with the island's musical heritage as the home of reggaetón, and the birthplace of the smash hit tune 'Despacito' and superstar musician Bad Bunny

programme.title: 50/50 Heroes
programme.desc: Animated comedy series. On her last test, Sam got an B+ instead of an A++, which lowers her grade average to a mere A. She loses sleep over this, has nightmares, and even forgets to perform her beloved Hammy's evening ritual. When Sam realises what a dreadful state she's in, her mind is made up to ask the Spectral Sceptre for the power to enter computers to alter her grade average in Mr Brick's computer. At half-power, Sam behaves like a computer virus - and infects her teacher's computer

programme.title: 50/50 Heroes
programme.desc: Animated comedy series. Mr Brick has been on edge for several days, handing out punishments left and right and piling on the assignments - what's bitten him? The answer comes in just two words - Mrs Uptight! She is the regional head of the educational project The School of the Future and is visiting the school - which is acting as a pilot scheme - to see Mr Brick give a demonstration. When Mr Brick flees the demonstration, Mo and Sam have to find a solution to get Mr Brick to return.

programme.title: 50/50 Heroes
programme.desc: Animated comedy series. Amber organises a special lunch event in the cafeteria featuring a chicory-based menu to rehabilitate the underrated vegetable - to avoid eating it, Mo tells Amber that he's highly sensitive to chicory. Amber is impressed that he becomes super-strong whenever he eats some - to the extent that he's actually dangerous to people around him - and pleads with him to give a public demonstration of his amazing strength during a musical Super-Chicory-Show that she's putting on.

Carrie delves into the legacy of Ambrose Congreve, a passionate gardener who entrusted Mt Congreve to the Irish state when he died in 2011. programme.title: Iománaíocht Hollywood
programme.sub-title: An Scéal sa mBaile (Gaelic Games in Irish Films)
programme.desc: Part two of two. The key role depictions of Gaelic games in the emergence of a distinctive Irish film culture. From the first indigenous feature film - Knocknagow (1918) - through the work of the National Film Institute of Ireland founded 1943, to recent Irish feature successes Lakelands (2022) and Róise and Frank (2022), Gaelic games have been a recurring and distinctive feature of Irish cinema

programme.sub-title: Gluons, Guacamole and the Colour Purple
programme.desc: When Sheldon is no longer academically challenged at Medford High School, he audits a college class taught by his pen pal Dr John Sturgis. Wallace Shawn and Michael Cudlitz guest star alongside Iain Armitage

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj lusts after a girl he and Sheldon meet at a university party, and Leonard comes to regret airing his disapproval of Penny's belief in psychics. Comedy, starring Kunal Nayyar and Jim Parsons

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Sheldon's flat is burgled, so they work on a state-of-the-art security system in a bid to outwit any future would-be thieves. However, their plans go awry when they ensnare Penny in a net. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon develops a mental block and has difficulty working on a formula, so in a move inspired by Albert Einstein he gets a job at the Cheesecake Factory diner to try to revive his creativity - and despite Penny's objections, proves surprisingly effective in the role. Guest starring Yeardley Smith (The voice of Lisa Simpson)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is invited to visit the Large Hadron Collider in Switzerland and asks Penny to join him as a Valentine's Day treat. However, Sheldon tries to persuade the couple to allow him to take her place

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon misses an opportunity to meet Spider-Man co-creator Stan Lee when he has to go to court for driving through a red light, and his anger leads to his imprisonment. Penny finds out the address of the comic-book writer and takes Sheldon there - but his troubles with the law have yet to reach their conclusion and the visit does not go as expected

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The friends fight over a ring they believe was used in the Lord of the Rings film trilogy. Leonard hopes to return the item to its rightful owner - but Raj wishes to sell it so he can buy a jet ski, and Sheldon develops a Gollum-like obsession with the precious object

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is awarded a prestigious prize by the university, but is scared of collecting the gong at a ceremony because he knows he'll have to give a speech. Penny tries to gee him up with rousing references to X-Men, and Leonard and Raj offer psychological guidance - without much success

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard tells Penny he loves her, but fails to get the response he would have liked. Sheldon also finds himself competing against Star Trek: The Next Generation actor Wil Wheaton once again when the gang goes bowling - and tries to get his revenge

programme.title: Georgie and Mandy's First Marriage
programme.desc: Audrey's past rears its head when Georgie leaves the tire store to work for Fred Fagenbacher, Jim's longtime nemesis.

programme.title: Georgie and Mandy's First Marriage
programme.desc: Georgie is forced to stay home with a cold. Young Sheldon spin-off, starring Montana Jordan and Emily Osment

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Dog, a Squirrel and a Fish Named Fish
programme.desc: A neighbourhood battle erupts between the Coopers and the Sparks family when the Sparks' new dog terrorises Sheldon. US comedy, starring Iain Armitage

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Summer Sausage, a Pocket Poncho and Tony Danza
programme.desc: While Sheldon is busy obsessing over the status of Meemaw's budding relationship with his mentor Dr John Sturgis, George and Mary attempt to bond with their other children. Comedy spin-off prequel to The Big Bang Theory, starring Iain Armitage

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Vanilla Ice-Cream, Gentlemen Callers and a Dinette Set
programme.desc: Meemaw is pursued by two gentlemen callers, only for Sheldon to interfere, forcing her to set some ground rules. Richard Kind and Wallace Shawn guest star, with Iain Armitage

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A High-Pitched Buzz and Training Wheels
programme.desc: The prodigy dismantles the refrigerator to stop it making an annoying humming noise and is then forced to get a paper round to pay for the repairs to the damaged appliance. Comedy spin-off prequel to The Big Bang Theory, starring Iain Armitage

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon tries to hide the fact he had dinner with Penny after she broke up with Leonard, hoping to stay friends with her. However, keeping secrets does not prove to be his strong point, and it isn't long before his deceit is exposed. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Kaley Cuoco and Johnny Galecki

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon invites a famous physicist to the flat after befriending her online, but is put out to find Leonard showing an interest in her. Meanwhile, Penny has to confront her ex-boyfriend when the visitor turns up in his bedroom. Guest starring Judy Greer (Glenn Martin, DDS)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The comedy flashes back to reveal how Sheldon saved the lives of his pals, and looks at the origins of his friendship with Leonard seven years previously, as well as explaining the housemates' agreement. Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj and Howard register Sheldon with an online dating site hoping to find his perfect female equivalent. Meanwhile, a drunk Penny seduces Leonard, but regrets it the next day. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco. Mayim Bialik makes her first appearance on the show

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny is stunned when Sheldon announces he is about to go on his first-ever date, and is even more surprised to find herself tagging along. Meanwhile, Leonard and Raj take a trip to the hospital after Howard's new robotic arm creates problems

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon tries to extend his lifespan, concerned he will not be around long enough for his consciousness to be downloaded into a robot body, and the friends encounter Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak (guest starring as himself) at the Cheesecake Factory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon breaks up with Amy and turns to cats in his search for an alternative to human companionship. However, when he starts to bulk-buy pets, Leonard decides enough is enough and calls for reinforcements to help bring his friend back to his senses. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: An embarrassing secret from Howard's past comes to light as he tries to win back Bernadette, while Sheldon and Raj engage in a battle of wills when they have to work in the same office. Guest starring Katee Sackhoff and George Takei

programme.title: Mock the Week: Summer Specials
programme.desc: Dara Ó Briain serves up more comedic aces with the help of guests Rhys James, Hugh Dennis, Sara Pascoe, Scott Bennett, Michelle Wolf and Glenn Moore.

programme.title: Blades of Glory (2007)
programme.desc: A bad-boy figure skater and his effeminate arch-enemy are banned from the sport after their intense rivalry results in a brawl. However, the pair find a loophole in the regulations allowing them to enter the world championships as the first ever same-sex pair - if they can put aside their differences long enough to compete. Comedy, starring Will Ferrell, Jon Heder, Will Arnett and Amy Poehler

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Carl has a suggestion for Mike that regular sex could be stifling Molly's creativity, as she reluctantly admits that she is suffering from writer's block. Starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Mike dresses up as Father Christmas, as he and Carl get into the festive spirit and hand out toys to children, while back at home, Molly has the family on edge. Starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Dr Sandra Lee treats Taylore, a young woman with unsightly elf-like skin growths on her ears. Jose has a baseball-sized lump on his head

programme.title: My 600lb Life
programme.desc: Krystal S wants to marry the man of her dreams, but she won't do it until she can be sure that she won't force him into the role of caretaker

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress
programme.desc: Bride-to-be Dina searches for a dress that is as sassy as she is. Meanwhile, Laure wants a simple lace gown for her big day, but the people around her have different ideas

programme.title: Loving Feeling (1968)
programme.desc: DJ Stevee Daly can't decide whether to fix his marriage to Suzanne or to yield to his many loving conquests. Stars Simon Brent and Georgina Ward. Contains nudity.

programme.title: Musketeer
programme.desc: An old musketeer meets a youth and two scoundrels on the road to Paris. Short drama, starring Kevin McNally, Andrew Lewis and Charlie Allen

programme.title: Die Screaming Marianne (1971)
programme.desc: Blackmailers try to reach a crooked judge through his daughter, a nightclub dancer in Portugal. Thriller, starring Barry Evans, Susan George and Judy Huxtable

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Fine art dealer Gavin Claxton, trader Linda Lambert and master upholsterer Anthony Devine head to Oxford to search for bargains

programme.desc: Teleshopping programming bringing you a selection of innovative products

programme.title: Cruise TV with LoveitBookit
programme.desc: Teleshopping feature bringing you the hottest destinations and latest deals to ensure your next cruise is your greatest one

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Flo Headlam design a garden to match Jo and Jack's Victorian terraced house. They have just £4,500 to tick all the boxes on the couple's extensive wishlist

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Flo Headlam and Lee Burkhill head to Yate, Gloucestershire, to create a Palma Majorca-inspired garden to remember Anna's husband, Shaun, who before his death from cancer was passionate about the garden, especially his vegetable patch and lawn

programme.title: Country House Auction
programme.desc: Newlywed couple Tessa and Robin juggle running a 48-bedroom castle and 50 acres of land with a special pair of Royal Highlander cows. In the auction room, a small but perfectly formed painting by one of the world's most famous artists, John Constable, is ready for auction. Silver enthusiast Oliver desperately hopes that a late 19th-century Japanese silver punchbowl will be coming home with him, while auctioneer Michael needs to sell a 1930s Philco radio that props-buyer Bridie and her mother Sara have fallen in love with

programme.title: Country House Auction
programme.desc: Egyptologist Dr Chris gets his hands on an inscribed Egyptian amulet and gives trainee auctioneer James a masterclass in ancient signs and symbols. A historical silver pin cushion, which was once owned by a leading lady of the Irish Arts and Crafts movement, piques the interest of competing buyers. Plus, auctioneer James gets up close and personal with the inner workings of some valuable watches

programme.title: Country House Auction
programme.desc: A mysterious pencil sketch of an anonymous couple by acclaimed artist Sean Keating enchants rival bidders Helen and David, but only auctioneer Michael's hammer can seal its fate. Gloster house owners and self-confessed auction addicts Tom and Mary are on the hunt for some antique gems for their 300-year-old home, while cabinetmaker Kieran restores a classic Victorian Cheval mirror for the sale

programme.title: Biggles: Adventure in Time (1986)
programme.desc: A New York businessman is mysteriously transported back in time to the First World War, where he meets the famed British flying ace Biggles. The time traveller discovers he has been sent back to help the daredevil pilot destroy a German secret weapon that could change the course of history. Sci-fi adventure, starring Neil Dickson, Alex Hyde-White and Peter Cushing in his final film role

programme.title: The Inglorious Bastards (1978)
programme.desc: Four American soldiers being transported to military prison through Nazi-occupied France escape when their convoy is attacked. Initially planning to escape to neutral Switzerland, they end up helping the French resistance steal a V2 rocket. Second World War thriller, starring Bo Svenson and Peter Hooten and Fred Williamson.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Flo Headlam design a garden to match Jo and Jack's Victorian terraced house. They have just £4,500 to tick all the boxes on the couple's extensive wishlist

programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.title: Let's Get Laid! (1977)
programme.desc: A former soldier is unwittingly drawn into the dangerous world of international espionage in postwar London. Saucy comedy, starring Robin Askwith, Fiona Richmond and Anthony Steel

programme.title: House of Whipcord (1974)
programme.desc: A French model befriends a strange man at a party and agrees to come home to meet his parents. She soon finds herself trapped in a disused prison taken over by a puritanical elderly couple, who seek to punish women whom they judge as immoral. Horror, starring Penny Irving, Barbara Markham, Patrick Barr and Ray Brooks

programme.title: The Magnificent Seven Deadly Sins (1971)
programme.desc: Anthology of seven comic tales, each based on one of the Seven Deadly Sins. Featuring the virtually silent tale of a tramp, a deadly vendetta, a rivalry between people from different social classes, and a lonely man haunting the London Underground in search of love. Comedy, starring Leslie Phillips, Spike Milligan, Harry Secombe and Bruce Forsyth

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Philip Serrell, Tony Wong and Micaela Sharp search Stratford-upon-Avon for items, choosing a bottle-corking machine, some lamps and old furniture in the hope of turning a profit

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Donna and Dan's garden is small and messy with lots of sharp difficult surfaces that present a problem for their youngest daughter Emilia, who has Down's syndrome. Emilia and her sister Maya love nothing more than playing in the garden, but currently it's unsafe and needs to be redesigned with more forgiving materials so they don't hurt themselves. Donna was born and raised in Norway, so Charlie Dimmock and Flo Headlam set about building a sensory, Scandi-themed garden within a £3,000 budget for the family to enjoy

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock returns to Fareham, Leicester and Shefford to see some past transformations and find out how her work has affected the lives of the garden owners. In Fareham, Hampshire, Vanessa and Alan have continued to improve on their Mediterranean wonder with olive trees, old stone walls and a giant Scots pine. A family in Leicester explains how their garden renovation has improved their lives and a tiny plot in Shefford has now become a multi-functional garden

programme.title: Our Dream Farm with Matt Baker
programme.desc: Matt Baker helps the National Trust find a new tenant for the vacant Wallington Estate farm in Northumberland and its 340 acres of grassland, woodland and wetland. Matt follows the process as seven applicants for the 10-year lease spend three weeks demonstrating they have what it takes to be chosen for the position. In this first episode, Matt arrives at the estate to meet the seven applicants. It's lambing season, so the hopefuls get straight to work

programme.title: Richard Wilson's Highland Fling
programme.desc: The actor explores the Highlands and islands of his native Scotland in grand style, from travelling by rail on the Belmond Royal Scotsman to cruising on the Hebridean Princess

programme.title: New Home DIY
programme.desc: Dave Wellman is in Shirley coming to the rescue of two newlyweds as he demonstrates how to repair their rotten floorboards

programme.title: New Home DIY
programme.desc: Dave is in Coventry, building a pair of his and hers wardrobes either side of a chimney breast in a master bedroom

programme.title: The Greatest Auction
programme.desc: Buyers bid for a guitar amp used by Jimi Hendrix and a bizarre collection of celebrity hair. An ornate chair with pagan carvings sparks a bidding war and a vintage travel trunk from the 1930s goes under the hammer

programme.title: Celebrity Coach Trip
programme.desc: The trip moves into its final week in Almancil in Portugal, and a fresh pair of arrivals kick off a dramatic and shocking twist that puts everyone's place on the coach at risk

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh comes to the aid of the Costellos in Norwich, who have recently moved into a bungalow with a garden in desperate need of a makeover. In 2009, dad Ian was injured by a roadside bomb while serving in Afghanistan, and has since been rehoused with wife Lyndsey and their three children to a property offered specifically to ex-service people and their families. The team aims to provide areas of relaxation, beauty and play, with David Domoney finding a way to stimulate the kids' imagination on a budget

programme.title: Our Dream Farm with Matt Baker
programme.desc: One pair of hopefuls become the first to spend a night in the farmhouse, with the responsibility of looking after the animals. As the temperature drops, the outside water pipes freeze, forcing them to fill buckets from the farmhouse for the livestock to drink. The National Trust set the hopefuls a task to prepare for the arrival of beavers into a 60-acre enclosure on the farm

programme.title: Strip Poker (1968)
programme.desc: Playboy John Carter is implicated in the murder of a blonde from a discotheque and is forced by gangsters into posing for pornographic photographs. (1968).

programme.title: Inside Out (1975)
programme.desc: A conman assembles a gang of crooks to retrieve a cache of gold bullion hidden in Germany since the end of the Second World War. However, the only person who knows its location is an imprisoned Nazi war criminal, so they first have to spring him from jail and then return him to his cell once they have the loot - without the authorities knowing. Comedy thriller, with Telly Savalas, James Mason, Robert Culp and Gunter Meisner

programme.title: Girl on a Motorcycle (1968)
programme.desc: A bored housewife leaves her husband and hits the open road to seek satisfaction in the arms of a former lover. Drama, starring Marianne Faithfull and Alain Delon

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The leader of a spiritual retreat is found strangled en route to the contemplation garden, giving Jack plenty to think about as he tries to figure out which member of the group was responsible. The problem is, they were all meditating at the time of the murder, and are able to provide one another with alibis, so how could any of them be the killer? To add to the DI's problems, he is struggling to come up with a speech to honour Commissioner Patterson at an award ceremony. Caribbean whodunit, starring Ardal O'Hanlon

programme.desc: Comedians Celya AB and Neil Delamere perform stand-up at the Hammersmith Apollo, hosted by Lou Sanders
