programme.desc: Money-saving expert Martin Lewis hosts the satirical quiz, with comedian Ignacio Lopez and broadcaster Steph McGovern joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton. Collectively, all concerned poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Modern Life Is Goodish
programme.desc: The comedian explores all aspects of light bulbs, gravy boats, dot-to-dot books and Lego as the programme taking a light-hearted look at modern life concludes

programme.title: Comedians Giving Lectures
programme.desc: Sara Pascoe curates the show that puts a novel spin on academia. Sara is joined by comedians Toussaint Douglass, Elf Lyons, and Frankie Boyle as they compete to deliver the best lecture

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Linda needs help shifting some heavy gear in Carmarthen, but it remains to be seen whether Scotty will help out. Sean Kelly presents

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: One unit in Cardiff has the bidders all gagging. Sam is determined not to be outbid by the other women and Scotty and John try to fathom why Heavy D has come dressed as a ninja

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: James May and Richard Hammond look at convertibles, comparing the VW Beetle, Citroen Pluriel, Mercedes CLK, Mazda MX5, Daihatsu Copen and Audi Cabriolet. Jeremy Clarkson puts the Nissan 350Z through its paces, and supermodel Jodie Kidd is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson finds a novel way to drive the Vauxhall Signum, General Motors' revolutionary Hy-wire concept car gets a test-drive and there's a look at Volkswagen's luxury limo the Phaeton. Actor Patrick Stewart gets up to warp speed on the racetrack as the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Motorhoming with Merton & Webster
programme.desc: Paul Merton and wife Suki Webster head for Somerset on the next leg of their grand UK tour in their motorhome - now christened Captain Narrow Squeak. Their first destination is Minehead, where they charter a fishing boat and, much to their surprise, land a small haul of fish for their supper - but they don't have the heart to keep them, so throw them all back

programme.title: Simon Reeve's South America
programme.desc: Simon starts his travels through Brazil in one of the remotest regions of the Amazon with the Waiapi people, who cling on to their traditional way of life, which is under threat from logging and mining interests. In Manaus, the city at the heart of the Amazon, he visits a neglected indigenous neighbourhood and meets a nurse who was the only source of healthcare during the pandemic. Simon ends this leg of his journey on the west coast in the city of Rio de Janeiro, where the government has built a hi-tech control room to monitor all parts of the city for potential disasters such as floods

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer return to Scotland for their latest fishing trip. Having failed to catch a salmon in the River Tay, they hope to have more luck on the Tweed. They begin their journey at the isolated Bemersyde Estate before moving on to the Bridge Pool at Kelso, one of the most famous salmon fishing spots in Scotland. They also try their hand at boat fishing and receive some mystical predictions from a familiar face

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer are joined by singer and conservationist Feargal Sharkey for a trip to the River Lea in Hertfordshire, where Paul used to fish for perch with his friends when he was a teenager. Bob reads up on the wise words of 17th-century angler Izaak Walton and prepares a riverside picnic that brings back fond memories and leads to a reflection on the differences between teenage life in their 1970s youth and the present. Setting up home in a narrowbooat, he also prepares a dish Paul's mother used to make - corned beef and potato pie

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer head to Norfolk, where they attempt to ensnare a large pike - a fish that has eluded them on their trips so far. As they arrive and get their gear together, Bob introduces Paul to the surprisingly varied contents of his car boot, much to Paul's amusement. They gather their equipment and find a decent spot that should give them plenty of chances to catch and make camp for the day. They end their trip with a visit to a local pub, where Bob reveals a hidden trick

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for brown trout on the river Test in Hampshire, using a Victorian fly-casting technique pioneered in the area as well as an authentic 19th-century rod. The Mayflies are hatching, and the famously short-lived insects inspire some thoughts on the ephemeral nature of life, and what they would do if they only had 24 hours left

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer go fishing for crucian carp in Wiltshire, a notoriously elusive fish that is usually only sighted at either dusk or dawn. They spend the night in a deconsecrated church, where Paul shares a new song that he is working on, while Bob brings a new fishing chair to the river that Paul does not approve of

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fish for chub on the river Wye in Herefordshire, Bob making an unexpected arrival by rowing boat. They are joined on today's trip by Dr Anand Patel from the Centre for Men's Health for a discussion of medical matters, and spend the night in a converted dovecote in the idyllic grounds of a 700-year-old manor house

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist ventures into uncharted territory in pursuit of new discoveries, encountering extraordinary wildlife and meeting remarkable people. To begin, Steve and a team of climbers and jungle survival experts head into the ancient landscape of South America's Guiana Shield. This vast wilderness of pristine forests and towering table-top mountains is one of the most inaccessible terrains on the planet

programme.title: Simon Reeve's South America
programme.desc: Simon starts his travels through Brazil in one of the remotest regions of the Amazon with the Waiapi people, who cling on to their traditional way of life, which is under threat from logging and mining interests. In Manaus, the city at the heart of the Amazon, he visits a neglected indigenous neighbourhood and meets a nurse who was the only source of healthcare during the pandemic. Simon ends this leg of his journey on the west coast in the city of Rio de Janeiro, where the government has built a hi-tech control room to monitor all parts of the city for potential disasters such as floods

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers find $6.5 million of cocaine in a secret compartment in a bus fuel tank, and a man is spotted putting something into his shoes - but what is he hiding?

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: Special police task forces tackle a brutal stabbing of a 16-year-old boy in Preston two days before Christmas, and Northamptonshire police target a drug-dealing gang on an estate

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Money-saving expert Martin Lewis hosts the satirical quiz, with comedian Ignacio Lopez and broadcaster Steph McGovern joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton. Collectively, all concerned poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry hosts the comedy panel game with regular Alan Davies and guests Liza Tarbuck, Jeremy Clarkson and Neil Mullarkey. In this episode the 'D' subjects include dogs

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and series regular Hugh Dennis poke fun at recent news in the company of Catherine Bohart, Ed Byrne, Darren Harriott, Rhys James and Felicity Ward

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and series regular Hugh Dennis look back on events taking place in May 2021 in the company of guests Maisie Adam, Angela Barnes, Rhys James, Robin Morgan and Ahir Shah

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedians Miles Jupp and Bob Mortimer and actresses Samantha Morton and Sarah Hadland join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants set out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory, through rounds such as Home Truths and Quick-fire Lies

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Maisie Adam hosts the comedy show filmed at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience before introducing stand-up routines by fellow comedians Michael Odewale and Susie McCabe

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended version of the quiz. Katy Brand, Sue Perkins and David Mitchell join regular panellist Alan Davies to answer Stephen Fry's obscure questions about people whose names begin with 'J'

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Modern Life Is Goodish
programme.sub-title: It Was an Accident and I Was Hospitalised
programme.desc: The presenter addresses so-called guilty pleasures and how some fans of things can take all of the fun out of liking something

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers intercept an 18-wheeler carrying a load of narcotics into Canada, and banned fruit and veterinary medicines are found hidden in a pick-up truck

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A wanted fugitive tries to escape as officers take her fingerprints, and a doctor entering America is caught smuggling in prescription medication worth thousands of dollars

programme.title: Rick Stein's Spain
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a culinary tour of the country, laced with history, literature and stunning photography. Driving a clapped-out old VW camper van around the Galicia region on the Atlantic coast, Rick discovers places off the beaten track as he samples classic dishes of rural Spain, a country he believes has been underrated in cookery circles until quite recently

programme.title: Rick Stein's Spain
programme.desc: The chef continues his culinary tour of Spain by travelling east from the Basque region toward the Mediterranean coast, sampling a selection of great dishes and enjoying local hospitality along the way

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson tackles the longest racetrack in the world, Germany's Nurburgring, and actor Christian Slater is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car. James May marks the 50th birthday of the Mercedes 300SL, one of the most revered cars ever made, and Richard Hammond celebrates 20 years of the Renault Espace

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond takes a look at the BMW 6 Series cabriolet, and Jeremy Clarkson explores whether the VW Golf GTI has gone back to its roots. There is an investigation to see if it is possible to purchase a Porsche for a snip, and Cliff Richard is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fly to Enniskillen, Co Fermanagh, to fish for pike, which eluded them in the previous series. They take to the waters of Lough Erne in their search for this mighty fish, guided by the very experienced Pascal. They discuss their respective bad habits and Bob gives Paul a thorough health update, before they retire to a local hostelry to sample traditional refreshments. Paul demonstrates how to perform life-saving CPR and Bob gives him a glimpse into his future by telling his fortune in an ancient Irish manner

programme.title: Simon Reeve's South America
programme.desc: The adventurer sets off on a journey through the continent, beginning in the remote and little-visited north-east. In Venezuela, Simon meets some of the thousands of migrants fleeing the economic collapse of their country, while he learns that neighbouring Guyana could be on the verge of its own oil boom. He also meets a gun-toting former warlord who is now one of Suriname's most successful businessmen, and ends his journey in French Guiana, where he goes to the launch site of the European Space Agency

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: It's been over a year since Frank's death and Wendy, rediscovering a long lost love for painting, decides to create a very unique tribute to him in oils. The usually curmudgeonly Geoffrey seems very keen on the commemorative artwork, but Lee and Lucy simply hate it. Once it's hanging on their wall, the pair contrive to 'lose' the painting without Wendy or Geoffrey noticing. Comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton, Deborah Grant and Geoffrey Whitehead

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee accidentally sends a rude text about Anna to Lucy, he makes the silly mistake of sending it to Anna herself. Lee and Lucy now face the impossible task of stealing Anna's phone and deleting the text before she read it. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton, with Abigail Cruttenden

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Modern Life Is Goodish
programme.desc: The comedian explores all aspects of light bulbs, gravy boats, dot-to-dot books and Lego as the programme taking a light-hearted look at modern life concludes

programme.title: Comedians Giving Lectures
programme.desc: Sara Pascoe curates the show that puts a novel spin on academia. Sara is joined by comedians Toussaint Douglass, Elf Lyons, and Frankie Boyle as they compete to deliver the best lecture

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Linda needs help shifting some heavy gear in Carmarthen, but it remains to be seen whether Scotty will help out. Sean Kelly presents

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: One unit in Cardiff has the bidders all gagging. Sam is determined not to be outbid by the other women and Scotty and John try to fathom why Heavy D has come dressed as a ninja

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: James May and Richard Hammond look at convertibles, comparing the VW Beetle, Citroen Pluriel, Mercedes CLK, Mazda MX5, Daihatsu Copen and Audi Cabriolet. Jeremy Clarkson puts the Nissan 350Z through its paces, and supermodel Jodie Kidd is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson finds a novel way to drive the Vauxhall Signum, General Motors' revolutionary Hy-wire concept car gets a test-drive and there's a look at Volkswagen's luxury limo the Phaeton. Actor Patrick Stewart gets up to warp speed on the racetrack as the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Motorhoming with Merton & Webster
programme.desc: Paul Merton and wife Suki Webster head for Somerset on the next leg of their grand UK tour in their motorhome - now christened Captain Narrow Squeak. Their first destination is Minehead, where they charter a fishing boat and, much to their surprise, land a small haul of fish for their supper - but they don't have the heart to keep them, so throw them all back

programme.title: Simon Reeve's South America
programme.desc: Simon starts his travels through Brazil in one of the remotest regions of the Amazon with the Waiapi people, who cling on to their traditional way of life, which is under threat from logging and mining interests. In Manaus, the city at the heart of the Amazon, he visits a neglected indigenous neighbourhood and meets a nurse who was the only source of healthcare during the pandemic. Simon ends this leg of his journey on the west coast in the city of Rio de Janeiro, where the government has built a hi-tech control room to monitor all parts of the city for potential disasters such as floods

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer return to Scotland for their latest fishing trip. Having failed to catch a salmon in the River Tay, they hope to have more luck on the Tweed. They begin their journey at the isolated Bemersyde Estate before moving on to the Bridge Pool at Kelso, one of the most famous salmon fishing spots in Scotland. They also try their hand at boat fishing and receive some mystical predictions from a familiar face

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer are joined by singer and conservationist Feargal Sharkey for a trip to the River Lea in Hertfordshire, where Paul used to fish for perch with his friends when he was a teenager. Bob reads up on the wise words of 17th-century angler Izaak Walton and prepares a riverside picnic that brings back fond memories and leads to a reflection on the differences between teenage life in their 1970s youth and the present. Setting up home in a narrowbooat, he also prepares a dish Paul's mother used to make - corned beef and potato pie

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer head to Norfolk, where they attempt to ensnare a large pike - a fish that has eluded them on their trips so far. As they arrive and get their gear together, Bob introduces Paul to the surprisingly varied contents of his car boot, much to Paul's amusement. They gather their equipment and find a decent spot that should give them plenty of chances to catch and make camp for the day. They end their trip with a visit to a local pub, where Bob reveals a hidden trick

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for brown trout on the river Test in Hampshire, using a Victorian fly-casting technique pioneered in the area as well as an authentic 19th-century rod. The Mayflies are hatching, and the famously short-lived insects inspire some thoughts on the ephemeral nature of life, and what they would do if they only had 24 hours left

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer go fishing for crucian carp in Wiltshire, a notoriously elusive fish that is usually only sighted at either dusk or dawn. They spend the night in a deconsecrated church, where Paul shares a new song that he is working on, while Bob brings a new fishing chair to the river that Paul does not approve of

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fish for chub on the river Wye in Herefordshire, Bob making an unexpected arrival by rowing boat. They are joined on today's trip by Dr Anand Patel from the Centre for Men's Health for a discussion of medical matters, and spend the night in a converted dovecote in the idyllic grounds of a 700-year-old manor house

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist ventures into uncharted territory in pursuit of new discoveries, encountering extraordinary wildlife and meeting remarkable people. To begin, Steve and a team of climbers and jungle survival experts head into the ancient landscape of South America's Guiana Shield. This vast wilderness of pristine forests and towering table-top mountains is one of the most inaccessible terrains on the planet

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne volunteers to put up four trainee doctors at Nonnatus House while they undertake the practical aspect of their obstetrics diploma, hoping the exercise will remind the council how vital the home is to the community and help remove the threat of demolition. There is a lot of excitement about the new arrivals and the doctors don't disappoint. On his CDC rounds, Fred comes across a street marked for demolition, with one house clearly showing signs of life. Period drama, starring Jenny Agutter

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: A school camp takes a deadly turn when a teacher is found swinging lifelessly from a zipline - apparently poisoned before the ride began. After the victim's past reveals a web of uneasy alliances and rivalries, Detective Shepherd must cipher through a long list of suspects. Drama, starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland and Jarod Rawiri

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A Sun Hill local brings a cool £500,000 into the nick, claiming to have found it dumped in the doorway of a hairdressing salon - arousing suspicion among the cynical coppers, who cannot bring themselves to believe anyone could be that honest. Police drama, starring Ben Roberts and Jeff Stewart

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Webb angers Cryer with his reluctance to take on the case of a body found on a roundabout, so the surly sergeant sets about identifying the man himself _ and proves experience is the other tenth of the law

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Boyden is shocked to discover someone impersonating him has been raiding illegal poker games and pocketing all the cash. Determined to stop the impudent villain from further blackening his reputation, he plans to go undercover as a punter. Starring Tony O'Callaghan

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of a three-part story. PC Smith reluctantly takes a seven-year-old Chinese boy under his wing after his parents disappear without explanation. Police drama, starring Alex Walkinshaw

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. The longer the search for Tim Tze's parents takes, the closer the bond becomes between the lad and his surrogate mentor PC Smith. Meanwhile, Deakin discovers the family's restaurant has been put up for sale and revelations about an extramarital affair come to light. Police drama, starring Shaun Scott, Alex Walkinshaw and Carl Tong

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne volunteers to put up four trainee doctors at Nonnatus House while they undertake the practical aspect of their obstetrics diploma, hoping the exercise will remind the council how vital the home is to the community and help remove the threat of demolition. There is a lot of excitement about the new arrivals and the doctors don't disappoint. On his CDC rounds, Fred comes across a street marked for demolition, with one house clearly showing signs of life. Period drama, starring Jenny Agutter

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Frances is at a loss when she cares for diabetic and recovering cancer patient Albert Calthorpe whose fiercely independent wife Grace refuses to accept further help, despite caring for her husband, her elderly mother and her pregnant daughter. Meanwhile, Violet is given two tickets to new hit film The Sound of Music and decides to raffle them off with all proceeds going to the incubator fund, while Timothy, Angela and May want to give their parents a night off, but they're not sure how. Samantha Spiro guest stars

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Secret
programme.desc: Feature-length dramatisation of the writer's only thriller, set in 19th-century Tyneside. Colin Buchanan stars as successful businessman Freddie Musgrave, who has worked hard for 20 years to live down his misspent youth as runner to a gang of smugglers and is shocked when a face appears from long ago, threatening to reveal the details of his sordid past. With Clare Higgins and June Whitfield

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: Miriam fears the consequences of David learning the truth, while Steph hides a secret that threatens the farm's future. Teresa's past comes back to haunt her in the shape of an unexpected visitor who could ruin her new life. Kate and Jack make plans for the future, and Laura's jealousy complicates her relationship with Richard. Erica struggles to save Bob and Pat's marriage, and Claire is caught in the crossfire between Frances and Joyce as plans get under way to build a new air raid shelter for the village

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The elderly sleuth makes a determined effort to drum up more business, and is hired to find a woman's missing son. She faces a race against time to track down the troubled youngster before he decides there's no point in going on. Detective drama, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Supermodel sensation Birdie reluctantly returns to her roots in Great Slaughter at the insistence of her money-obsessed manager, Max Savage. When he is found dead in a locked room, Sister Boniface and the team are called in to investigate

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki and Jack attend the scene of a fatal car crash in which a driver lost control of her vehicle and the forensic evidence suggests it may not have been an accident and that the victim's son Kevin McDowd - a resident of Kingscote Bows care home - could be involved in the death. The teams begin to notice a pattern of foul play and a connection between the care home and other suspicious deaths that had previously gone unnoticed, while Kevin absconds and takes his friend Serena with him

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra and the team investigate why five years earlier a man seemingly vanished during his morning run and has not been seen since. The alarm was raised when he never made it to work at the construction company belonging to his father-in-law, who has a history of violence, and the detectives' suspicions grow when they realise how much the missing man's wife is scared of her dad. Could he have had something to do with his son-in-law's disappearance? Amanda Redman, Nicholas Lyndhurst, Denis Lawson and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: 2point4 Children
programme.desc: Ben leaves Bill in the company of her sister-in-law and the two have a row. Furious, Bill takes to the road before realising that technically she cannot drive, but a familiar face in the form of biker Angelo soon comes to her aid. Domestic comedy, guest starring Sandra Dickinson

programme.title: The Boy That Never Was
programme.desc: Three years after losing their son in an earthquake in Morocco, a couple's world is turned upside down when they spot a young boy in Dublin who they believe to be him. After losing sight of the youngster, they set out on a mission to search for the truth and find him. Drama, starring Colin Morgan and Toni O'Rourke

programme.title: 2point4 Children
programme.sub-title: When the Going Gets Tough, the Tough Go Shopping
programme.desc: Bill decides to become a vegetarian and goes in search of lentils. However, problems ensue when she gives chase to a thief, and is mistakenly arrested herself. Meanwhile, Ben is more concerned about a letter from the taxman. Comedy, starring Belinda Lang and Gary Olsen

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Mac worries about an old friend who appears to be well and truly in the clutches of a buxom blonde gold-digger, and Katrina comes to blows with Marc over his bedside manner. programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Vivien visits a friend whose husband - a lecturer in Saxon history - has recently died. When his Norwegian girlfriend turns up and steals his ashes to fulfil his last wish of a Viking funeral, the nurse tries to help the women reach a compromise. Meanwhile, Ronnie calls Vivien with good news about Ryan and Lee's sentences. Anita Carey stars

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: It's the day of Owen's court hearing, where he hopes to gain regular access to his daughter - but he turns up late after an emergency and is accused of being too impulsive by Lauren. Mark celebrates his birthday and Chrissie prepares a surprise reunion with his father, though things don't work out as planned. Zubin takes to parenthood like a duck to water, but Jess is finding it hard to cope with looking after the baby

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Sam refuses to talk to Keith Parr about his neck injury, and instead focuses on treating a patient she believes was sexually assaulted. Meanwhile, a woman is admitted to the ED after being struck by a lorry, and Lloyd jumps to conclusions when the driver reveals he drank alcohol the night before. Linda has a chance to reassess her feelings when Lenny questions her about putting her niece and nephew into care. Guest starring Jenny Platt (Coronation Street's Violet Wilson) and Jacquetta May (EastEnders' Rachel Kominski), with Charlotte Salt and Michael Obiora

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: In the conclusion of the three-part story, kidnappers snatch Tim, but not before Smithy cuffs himself to the terrified child, while officers elsewhere discover his missing parents are embroiled in a million-pound money laundering racket. Police drama, starring Alex Walkinshaw, Shaun Scott and Simon Rouse.

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Carol faces the worst day of her life when she hears that her son is dead

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Carol blames Lauren for Billie's death but Max turns on her and gives her some home truths. Meanwhile Jay chooses Phil over Billy and Liam struggles to keep his secret

programme.title: Tenko
programme.desc: The frustration of living in a prison camp drives Blanche to take desperate measures. Second World War drama, starring Ann Bell and Stephanie Beacham

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Henry is enlisted in the search for a missing policeman, but the restaurant's opening night is looming. Detective drama, starring Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer returns from six months in Spain to find a guest of Lady Jane has fled Peru with a priceless Inca ring. Joanna Lumley guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: A misguided attempt to injure the gardener at Bayview lands Diana in hospital with a broken hip and it looks like she might miss her niece's wedding. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The delinquent pensioners join Barry and Wesley to help rescue a couple whose car is bogged down in a remote field - but the woman surprises them by revealing she has mystic powers and declares Barry is the chosen one destined to marry her. Comedy, starring Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Nora dons a revealing outfit for a church pageant and is dared by Ivy to call Compo's bluff and present herself on his doorstep. Unfortunately, the shock proves too much for him and he is raced to hospital. Alas, he failed to make it through, presenting his friends with a dilemma on how best to commemorate him. Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton star in a special instalment of Roy Clarke's comedy, marking the departure of Bill Owen's character from the series

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: As preparations get under way for Compo's funeral, Clegg is amazed to be given a letter written by the old rascal with strict instructions that it should only be opened in event of his death. Peter Sallis, Kathy Staff and Frank Thornton star

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The grand reopening of a vintage railway in Brokenwood comes to a grinding halt when a bride is found dead on the tracks on her wedding day

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra and the team investigate why five years earlier a man seemingly vanished during his morning run and has not been seen since. The alarm was raised when he never made it to work at the construction company belonging to his father-in-law, who has a history of violence, and the detectives' suspicions grow when they realise how much the missing man's wife is scared of her dad. Could he have had something to do with his son-in-law's disappearance? Amanda Redman, Nicholas Lyndhurst, Denis Lawson and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A preserved female body is found buried in a Sheffield peat moor, and DI Sonia Hardwick calls in archaeologist Patrick Cain to help with the investigation, assisted by Nikki. When an examination of the corpse suggests a sexually motivated attack, Leo suspects there is a link to a serial killer case he dealt with in 1985, but following the lead means returning to his home town and confronting the memories of his dead wife and child. Guest starring Mark Benton and Barbara Flynn, with William Gaminara

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The roguish antiques dealer returns from six months in Spain to find a guest of Lady Jane has fled Peru with a priceless Inca ring. Joanna Lumley guest stars, with Ian McShane

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Midnight in the Garden of Good & Ungood
programme.desc: A mysterious recluse - and rumoured ex-spy - hosts a New Year's Eve masquerade ball, but his untimely death turns masked guests into suspects

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: Animals that travel in and out of cities in search of food or a safe place for their young. African penguins have found the streets of Cape Town a safe environment from predators, while still providing easy access to their fishing grounds, while in St Lucia, hippos have developed a taste for eating residents' lawns. Black bears roam the streets of cities across North America, seeking out discarded food to help them consume the 20,000 calories a day they need to survive their six-month hibernation

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Mrs Chambers takes it on herself to ensure her daughter's wedding is fit for a queen, but her over-the-top ideas soon meet with disapproval. Stirred up by Terry's ridicule, the men rebel

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: On the eve of his wedding, Bob turns down the chance of going on a drunken rampage in favour of a quiet night in. Classic comedy, starring Rodney Bewes and James Bolam

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: As Bob's wedding day dawns, a frustrated Terry continues to wreak havoc on people who happen to be unfortunate enough to live near him. Comedy, starring Rodney Bewes and James Bolam

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob and Thelma escape from Terry's influence as they begin their married life with a honeymoon in Norway, while Terry becomes acquainted with Thelma's sister Susan back at home

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob and Terry decide to chat up a couple of girls in a restaurant to see if they are still attractive to the opposite sex. Comedy, starring James Bolam and Rodney Bewes

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Ben Robinson explores Parkgate on the estuary of the River Dee, Cheshire. The village is landlocked due to the estuary's shifting silt, but 250 years ago it was a bustling harbour

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: New Zealand is now home to some of the richest agricultural land in the world, boasting magnificent gardens with plants from around the planet and rare magical creatures. A caving expedition reveals relics of the original lost world of its pre-human past; the actual bones of a cow-sized flightless grazing bird, a moa, among the largest birds that has ever lived. However, as narrator Sam Neill reveals, against all the odds, one giant bird from primeval New Zealand has survived to this day

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: From pythons in Bangkok to otters in Singapore, this documenary explores the extraordinary ways wildlife is making itself at home in the world's cities

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: Animals that travel in and out of cities in search of food or a safe place for their young. African penguins have found the streets of Cape Town a safe environment from predators, while still providing easy access to their fishing grounds, while in St Lucia, hippos have developed a taste for eating residents' lawns. Black bears roam the streets of cities across North America, seeking out discarded food to help them consume the 20,000 calories a day they need to survive their six-month hibernation

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: Animals displaced from their habitats by human expansion, including elephants in Sri Lanka and capuchin monkeys on the Pacific coast of Costa Rica. The programme also examines situations where humans have learned to live alongside their animal neighbours, including a heron that has been visiting an Amsterdam house twice a day for 17 years and a project to preserve manatees in Florida that has saved them from being an endangered species

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian travels along one of the world's most contentious borders, visiting places in America and Mexico to find out what life is like on both sides. Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she helps Honduran volunteers building a hostel for fellow refugees fleeing violence in their country

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson unearths the Suffolk village of Orford's history of defending the British Isles, and uncovers a local legend involving smugglers and the local pub

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A maintenance team attempts to overhaul a finicky gearbox while another team must shunt an electric passenger train across multiple rail crossings during peak hour

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A rusty 10-ton water tank gets cut off of a vintage engine in the Adelaide Hills, and Kuranda Scenic Railway carriages get spruced up by the Queensland Rail carpentry team

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: In Melbourne, the busy SCT maintenance team expect the unexpected every day as they provision, inspect and shunt engines in and out of a bustling maintenance pit

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: The crew at Downer Rail in Sydney fix a leaking air conditioner while QR's Rail Operations Response Unit remove debris from high voltage train power lines

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob is disheartened by some of the responsibilities of married life, including having to work longer shifts, worrying endlessly and doing lots of DIY, while Terry is free to spend his time at the bookies and in the pub. First shown in 1974

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob is arrested for drink-driving after going out for dinner with Thelma. At the police station he meets up with Terry, who has been collared for misbehaving at a football match, and the two agree to swap blood samples. Classic comedy, starring Rodney Bewes, James Bolam and Brigit Forsyth

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob and Terry show their competitive spirit when they challenge each other to a cross-country cycle race. Comedy, starring Rodney Bewes, James Bolam and Brigit Forsyth

African penguins have found the streets of Cape Town a safe environment from predators, while still providing easy access to their fishing grounds, while in St Lucia, hippos have developed a taste for eating residents' lawns. Black bears roam the streets of cities across North America, seeking out discarded food to help them consume the 20,000 calories a day they need to survive their six-month hibernation

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob is disheartened by some of the responsibilities of married life, including having to work longer shifts, worrying endlessly and doing lots of DIY, while Terry is free to spend his time at the bookies and in the pub. First shown in 1974

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: New Zealand is now home to some of the richest agricultural land in the world, boasting magnificent gardens with plants from around the planet and rare magical creatures. A caving expedition reveals relics of the original lost world of its pre-human past; the actual bones of a cow-sized flightless grazing bird, a moa, among the largest birds that has ever lived. However, as narrator Sam Neill reveals, against all the odds, one giant bird from primeval New Zealand has survived to this day

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: From pythons in Bangkok to otters in Singapore, this documenary explores the extraordinary ways wildlife is making itself at home in the world's cities

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: Animals that travel in and out of cities in search of food or a safe place for their young. African penguins have found the streets of Cape Town a safe environment from predators, while still providing easy access to their fishing grounds, while in St Lucia, hippos have developed a taste for eating residents' lawns. Black bears roam the streets of cities across North America, seeking out discarded food to help them consume the 20,000 calories a day they need to survive their six-month hibernation

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: Animals displaced from their habitats by human expansion, including elephants in Sri Lanka and capuchin monkeys on the Pacific coast of Costa Rica. The programme also examines situations where humans have learned to live alongside their animal neighbours, including a heron that has been visiting an Amsterdam house twice a day for 17 years and a project to preserve manatees in Florida that has saved them from being an endangered species

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian travels along one of the world's most contentious borders, visiting places in America and Mexico to find out what life is like on both sides. Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she helps Honduran volunteers building a hostel for fellow refugees fleeing violence in their country

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson unearths the Suffolk village of Orford's history of defending the British Isles, and uncovers a local legend involving smugglers and the local pub

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A maintenance team attempts to overhaul a finicky gearbox while another team must shunt an electric passenger train across multiple rail crossings during peak hour

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: A rusty 10-ton water tank gets cut off of a vintage engine in the Adelaide Hills, and Kuranda Scenic Railway carriages get spruced up by the Queensland Rail carpentry team

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: In Melbourne, the busy SCT maintenance team expect the unexpected every day as they provision, inspect and shunt engines in and out of a bustling maintenance pit

programme.title: Train Rescue Down Under
programme.desc: The crew at Downer Rail in Sydney fix a leaking air conditioner while QR's Rail Operations Response Unit remove debris from high voltage train power lines

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Back from honeymoon, Bob and Thelma become increasingly worried about Terry, who seems to have gone off beer and mellowed into a strange state of middle-aged contemplativeness. Rodney Bewes and James Bolam star

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?

Classic comedy, starring Rodney Bewes, James Bolam and Brigit Forsyth

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike discover their neighbour has been scheming behind their backs - his outlandish tactics threatening the success of their guesthouse business. For Alison, this warrants all out war and she sets about playing him at his own game. If she is going to stoop to such trickery however, she'll need the help of one of the ghosts. Mike attempts to mix things up for future guests at the gatehouse by secretly investing in some not-so-cheap entertainment that Alison will definitely not approve of. Meanwhile, the Captain makes a discovery, leading the other ghosts to find a surprising new friend

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: First episode of Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French as the new vicar of a sleepy English village. Her arrival causes consternation among the locals - particularly stuffy councillor David Horton, who vows to have her ejected at all costs. Gary Waldhorn, James Fleet and Emma Chambers co-star

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The Songs of Praise crew visits Dibley to record a programme in the village church. However, when Geraldine develops a crush on the producer her wayward emotions threaten to plunge the proceedings into disaster. Comedy, guest starring Peter Capaldi (The Thick of It)

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine is keen to find a celebrity to open the autumn fair in style - and Alice inadvertently comes up with the ideal candidate by letting slip she is related to one Reg Dwight - AKA Elton John. Comedy, starring Dawn French, and Emma Chambers with a guest appearance by Kylie Minogue. First shown in 1994

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A violent storm destroys St Barnabas's stained glass window, and Geraldine is put in charge of raising funds to replace it - but the others are unsure whether any of her outlandish ideas will actually work. Comedy, starring Dawn French, Gary Waldhorn, James Fleet, Emma Chambers and Liz Smith

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Local election fever hits the village and Geraldine finds she is a more popular candidate with the voters than over-confident parish council chairman David. Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.desc: Alison and Mike discover their neighbour has been scheming behind their backs - his outlandish tactics threatening the success of their guesthouse business. For Alison, this warrants all out war and she sets about playing him at his own game. If she is going to stoop to such trickery however, she'll need the help of one of the ghosts. Mike attempts to mix things up for future guests at the gatehouse by secretly investing in some not-so-cheap entertainment that Alison will definitely not approve of. Meanwhile, the Captain makes a discovery, leading the other ghosts to find a surprising new friend

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike receive some exciting news about their guesthouse business, but the fallout soon sees tensions running high between the pair. programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Nick brings home an old, broken-down vehicle he won in a poker game, intending to break it up for parts. However, Ben decides to restore it to its former glory in an attempt to rekindle his youth. The trouble is, it is his wedding anniversary and Susan wants to spend the weekend away

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben is disappointed that his request for a no-fuss birthday is taken a little too literally. Meanwhile, Janey's weekend visit turns out to be a very revealing one when she sits her parents down to tell them the truth about her new life at university

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: With the children away for the weekend, Ben and Susan have the chance to spice up their love life - but he just wants to laze around the house while she is keen to go shopping for antiques. As they search for joint interests, they discover all they share is the need to descale the toilet

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Michael and Abi discover the house is cursed, although the revelation fails to have any effect on Ben. Almost 28 years since Kirk Mason became the first man ever to stand Susan up, he has got back in touch, little suspecting the woman he snubbed could still be holding a grudge

programme.title: My Family
programme.sub-title: One Flew Out of the Cuckoo's Nest
programme.desc: For once, it seems it's Ben's dreams rather than his nightmares that are coming true, when Nick finally announces plans to move out of the family home. And, if that's not enough good news, the elder son also has a job - albeit at his dad's local pub

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Family members rush their chihuahua, Darcy, to the clinic after it is hit by a car. For 18-year-old TaJournée, who is autistic, the dog is a lifeline, and Noel must use pins, nuts and bolts to reconstruct the broken pieces of its crushed pelvis. Meanwhile, the vet finds himself in a tricky situation while performing replacement surgery on a seven-year-old spaniel with hip problems, and a Winchester Prison sniffer dog that is lame in both elbows is brought to the surgery

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A one-year-old St Bernard suffering with hip dysplasia is brought to the clinic by the Warden family. Having recently lost the the dog's brother to a similar illness, emotions are running high. Meanwhile, Noel treats a 13-year-old collie that has been hit by a car, and tries to devise a new bionic paw for a cat with a badly deformed hind foot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A four-year-old chinchilla arrives for surgery on a broken leg, but his tiny airways and bones make it a difficult procedure. Meanwhile, a puppy is rushed to the veterinary practice after being hit by a van, and Noel must deal with the risk of infection as he fights to save its limb. Plus, a Labrador undergoes an awkward operation for two bulging discs

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel tries to fix a cocker spaniel's leg problems by fitting two bionic back feet, before launching a series of investigations into what is causing a cat to drag its leg. Plus, a Westie puppy with a fractured knee arrives, but it cannot keep still

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A woman who suffers from nocturnal epileptic fits brings in Pearl, a Staffordshire bull terrier that can sense when these episodes are about to happen. However, the dog has problems with its knees and struggles to walk, so the pressure is on for Noel Fitzpatrick to restore it back to full health. Plus, an Irish setter has a cancerous tumour in its front leg, and a cat needs its jaw wiring back together after being hit by a car

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The paramedics race to a lorry driver with a branch through his windscreen and a man with an irregular heartbeat, and help a family faced with a heart-breaking decision

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics treat a woman trapped in her car after a crash, a man with a hernia and a sick 3-year-old. Plus, they're called to an elderly woman who has badly cut her legs

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: A seven-year-old has an operation to straighten her squint and an ex-Army man, who served in Afghanistan and Iraq, has a procedure to get rid of constant back pain

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Anne needs an operation to remove a painful bony lump from her foot and mother-of-four Laura decides to have a sterilisation after suffering from post-natal depression

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Leslie Phillips and Olympic curling champion Rhona Martin guest star in the comedy sketch show, featuring the comedienne as an assortment of quirky characters. Among other highlights, Nan visits an old friend in hospital and Lauren takes a job in a burger bar

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Jools Holland and pop band the Divine Comedy guest star in the comedy sketch show, featuring the comedienne as an assortment of quirky and bizarre characters. Lauren fights with Kelly over Ryan, and 1950s landlady Ma Willow makes her first appearance

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick faces one of his greatest practical and ethical challenges yet when he's introduced to Hermes, a tortoise with only one good leg. Can he provide Hermes with three prosthetic feet, using tiny titanium implants? Meanwhile, a rottweiler called Dave urgently needs an upgrade to his previous hip replacements

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Sleeps Over USA
programme.desc: We head to Nevada, the only US state where brothels are legal, to see if places like The Mustang Ranch should exist as a solution to the dangers sex workers face

programme.desc: The Death Cleaners help Lindsey after her mother and extended family leave everything they don't want at her house - causing her to lose her personal style

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Swansea to meet 15-year-old Jack, who suffers from a rare form of muscular dystrophy that has led to a lifetime of painful operations. His mother Helen and older brother Daniel do all they can to help but as he grows it has become impossible to get him upstairs and now he has to sleep in the living room, leaving the rest of the family with nowhere to socialise. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team turns up to transform their three-bedroom semi, backed up by an army of local tradespeople, with designer Gabrielle Blackman on hand to ensure they don't stray too far from her vision

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Dartford to meet Scott and Jenny, whose third son Charlie was born with cerebral palsy and developmental delay. The boy needs round-the-clock care, which his mother gave up work to provide, but now he is 13, the family are struggling to cope in their small home. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team turns up to transform their end terrace, backed up by an army of local tradespeople, with designer Naomi Cleaver on hand to ensure they don't stray too far from her vision

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and his crack team of builders, plasterers and electricians come to the aid of Frank and Christine Burrows in Wigan, Greater Manchester. In 2012, the Burrows' 35-year-old son Mark retrained as a scaffolder to follow in his father's footsteps, but was paralysed as the result of an unprovoked attack that left him with severe brain damage. Mark now needs constant care, but the Burrows' family home is not yet fit for purpose, so Frank and Christine have been forced to take their son to a care home 15 miles away. With the help of flamboyant designer Laurence Llewelyn Bowen, Nick's experts set about constructing a self-contained apartment alongside the Burrows' two-bedroom bungalow, which will provide Mark with the space, independence and security he will need to return home for good after spending two years away

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Paul Laidlaw scour Somerset for valuables and head for auction in Hampshire, with Paul's buys including items from America, Africa and Staffordshire

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw and Kate Bliss go head-to-head at an auction in Windsor, where Paul comes across a tank, and Kate hopes to make a profit with a mechanical penguin

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Marbella-based seven-month-old expat French bulldog is flown in by private jet from Spain with his family after surgery to fix his broken elbow proved unsuccessful. Its family hopes a custom fusion plate system will get Milo out of pain. Eleven-year-old rescue brindle boxer Bramble is referred to Noel by a local vet after a mystery mass has been found eating away the bone around her knee. Owner Paula opts for an innovative total knee replacement, but Noel is then presented with a massive dilemma when the hound breaks her femur during recovery

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick steps out of his comfort zone by heading to South Africa to spend time with the magnificent animals of the savannah and the selfless people who dedicate their lives to looking after them - often in very difficult circumstances. Noel meets the animals threatened by poaching, injured due to neglect in captivity, and exploited in one way or another. The pioneering vet also helps endangered rhinos and improve the lives of two big cats by performing procedures rarely - if ever - provided for wild animals before

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Swansea to meet 15-year-old Jack, who suffers from a rare form of muscular dystrophy that has led to a lifetime of painful operations. His mother Helen and older brother Daniel do all they can to help but as he grows it has become impossible to get him upstairs and now he has to sleep in the living room, leaving the rest of the family with nowhere to socialise. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team turns up to transform their three-bedroom semi, backed up by an army of local tradespeople, with designer Gabrielle Blackman on hand to ensure they don't stray too far from her vision

programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Dartford to meet Scott and Jenny, whose third son Charlie was born with cerebral palsy and developmental delay. The boy needs round-the-clock care, which his mother gave up work to provide, but now he is 13, the family are struggling to cope in their small home. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team turns up to transform their end terrace, backed up by an army of local tradespeople, with designer Naomi Cleaver on hand to ensure they don't stray too far from her vision

programme.title: Aaron Hernandez Uncovered

programme.desc: Janine and Adam assist a woman who is threatening to jump out of a first-floor window, and husband and wife team Ann and Kevin are called out to help Ann's sick Dad

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Janey is horrified when Susan visits her at the university's halls of residence and tries to relive her student days. Ben's round of golf with his old friend Gerald turns into a disaster, and Nick reveals his latest money-making venture. From October 2002

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Nick brings home an old, broken-down vehicle he won in a poker game, intending to break it up for parts. However, Ben decides to restore it to its former glory in an attempt to rekindle his youth. The trouble is, it is his wedding anniversary and Susan wants to spend the weekend away

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben is disappointed that his request for a no-fuss birthday is taken a little too literally. Meanwhile, Janey's weekend visit turns out to be a very revealing one when she sits her parents down to tell them the truth about her new life at university

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: With the children away for the weekend, Ben and Susan have the chance to spice up their love life - but he just wants to laze around the house while she is keen to go shopping for antiques. As they search for joint interests, they discover all they share is the need to descale the toilet

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Michael and Abi discover the house is cursed, although the revelation fails to have any effect on Ben. Almost 28 years since Kirk Mason became the first man ever to stand Susan up, he has got back in touch, little suspecting the woman he snubbed could still be holding a grudge

programme.title: My Family
programme.sub-title: One Flew Out of the Cuckoo's Nest
programme.desc: For once, it seems it's Ben's dreams rather than his nightmares that are coming true, when Nick finally announces plans to move out of the family home. And, if that's not enough good news, the elder son also has a job - albeit at his dad's local pub

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Family members rush their chihuahua, Darcy, to the clinic after it is hit by a car. For 18-year-old TaJournée, who is autistic, the dog is a lifeline, and Noel must use pins, nuts and bolts to reconstruct the broken pieces of its crushed pelvis. Meanwhile, the vet finds himself in a tricky situation while performing replacement surgery on a seven-year-old spaniel with hip problems, and a Winchester Prison sniffer dog that is lame in both elbows is brought to the surgery

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A one-year-old St Bernard suffering with hip dysplasia is brought to the clinic by the Warden family. Having recently lost the the dog's brother to a similar illness, emotions are running high. Meanwhile, Noel treats a 13-year-old collie that has been hit by a car, and tries to devise a new bionic paw for a cat with a badly deformed hind foot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A four-year-old chinchilla arrives for surgery on a broken leg, but his tiny airways and bones make it a difficult procedure. Meanwhile, a puppy is rushed to the veterinary practice after being hit by a van, and Noel must deal with the risk of infection as he fights to save its limb. Plus, a Labrador undergoes an awkward operation for two bulging discs

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel tries to fix a cocker spaniel's leg problems by fitting two bionic back feet, before launching a series of investigations into what is causing a cat to drag its leg. Plus, a Westie puppy with a fractured knee arrives, but it cannot keep still

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A woman who suffers from nocturnal epileptic fits brings in Pearl, a Staffordshire bull terrier that can sense when these episodes are about to happen. However, the dog has problems with its knees and struggles to walk, so the pressure is on for Noel Fitzpatrick to restore it back to full health. Plus, an Irish setter has a cancerous tumour in its front leg, and a cat needs its jaw wiring back together after being hit by a car

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The paramedics race to a lorry driver with a branch through his windscreen and a man with an irregular heartbeat, and help a family faced with a heart-breaking decision

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics treat a woman trapped in her car after a crash, a man with a hernia and a sick 3-year-old. Plus, they're called to an elderly woman who has badly cut her legs

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: A seven-year-old has an operation to straighten her squint and an ex-Army man, who served in Afghanistan and Iraq, has a procedure to get rid of constant back pain

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Anne needs an operation to remove a painful bony lump from her foot and mother-of-four Laura decides to have a sterilisation after suffering from post-natal depression

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Leslie Phillips and Olympic curling champion Rhona Martin guest star in the comedy sketch show, featuring the comedienne as an assortment of quirky characters. Among other highlights, Nan visits an old friend in hospital and Lauren takes a job in a burger bar

programme.desc: Jools Holland and pop band the Divine Comedy guest star in the comedy sketch show, featuring the comedienne as an assortment of quirky and bizarre characters. Lauren fights with Kelly over Ryan, and 1950s landlady Ma Willow makes her first appearance

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and his crack team of builders, plasterers and electricians come to the aid of Frank and Christine Burrows in Wigan, Greater Manchester. In 2012, the Burrows' 35-year-old son Mark retrained as a scaffolder to follow in his father's footsteps, but was paralysed as the result of an unprovoked attack that left him with severe brain damage. Mark now needs constant care, but the Burrows' family home is not yet fit for purpose, so Frank and Christine have been forced to take their son to a care home 15 miles away. With the help of flamboyant designer Laurence Llewelyn Bowen, Nick's experts set about constructing a self-contained apartment alongside the Burrows' two-bedroom bungalow, which will provide Mark with the space, independence and security he will need to return home for good after spending two years away

programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Paul Laidlaw scour Somerset for valuables and head for auction in Hampshire, with Paul's buys including items from America, Africa and Staffordshire

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Marbella-based seven-month-old expat French bulldog is flown in by private jet from Spain with his family after surgery to fix his broken elbow proved unsuccessful. programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick faces one of his greatest practical and ethical challenges yet when he's introduced to Hermes, a tortoise with only one good leg. programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Paul Laidlaw's final trip in the 1960s Volvo sees them plot a course through Kent. The duo later head for an auction in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk

programme.desc: Paul and Charlie head south for an immaculate Lotus Super 7. Derek and Jack are in Sheffield collecting a 1929 Riley Monaco and a 1924 Alvis 12/50 tourer

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: On the way to pick up two lovely '70s Lotus sports cars, Derek takes to the skies for his first ever helicopter flight in a 1967 Bell 47 chopper

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Dave's drawn to a Fiat Uno 45 in a '1970s beige' with 36,000 miles on the clock

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Paul hits the road to collect an old navy ambulance that served its time as a holiday camper

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Our Guy in China
programme.desc: Guy Martin explores Beijing, ignoring the tourist traps and following his own passions as he reveals a lesser-seen side of China. He meets families who live on a scrapyard and earn their livings by processing old cars and spends an afternoon helping out by operating the cranes and machinery, before visiting the hub of the market for rare and exotic supercar sales and assists in the building of what will be the tallest building in Beijing, the China Zun skyscraper

programme.title: Our Guy in China
programme.desc: Guy Martin concludes his Chinese adventure by taking on his toughest-ever physical challenge as he sets to break the record for the fastest crossing of the Taklamakan Desert on a bicycle. He must travel 341 miles along the world's longest desert highway in less than 47 hours and as extreme fatigue sets in, he starts to nod off while riding, swerving off the edge of the road. Stopping in a lay-by for food and rest, he can't sleep because of his overactive mind and decides to keep riding through the night as he heads for the finish line in Qiemo

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits Yosemite National Park in California, beholding its towering granite cliffs, giant sequoia trees and cascading waterfalls. He then heads to the University of California's seismology department to hear about the devastating 1906 San Francisco earthquake, which claimed the lives of approximately 3,000 people and destroyed more than 80 per cent of the city

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues through southern California aboard the Coast Starlight rail service. In the port of Monterey, he discovers the city's Spanish and Mexican heritage, before heading to Paso de Robles, where he tours Hearst Castle, dream home of the newspaper baron, William Randolph Hearst. In the hills of Morro Bay, Michael joins avocado farmer, Jim Shanley, amid his 4,000 avocado trees to harvest the pears and learn how to make guacamole. Arriving in Los Angeles, Michael heads for the Warner Brothers studio, founded on Sunset Boulevard

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his tour of Los Angeles, joining the locals for Mexican Independence Day, and discovering the secrets of backyard oil drilling in a county that is home to the largest urban oil field in the US. Moving on to Laguna Beach, he learns how artists from the east coast travelled west on the Transcontinental Railroad to found a colony of painters attracted by the beautiful coastline and glorious light to paint outside

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie helps rescue a runaway narrowboat on the scenic Macclesfield Canal and cruises through autumn scenery toward Congleton to see a shipwreck up for sale

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Fallen trees block Robbie's journey on the Peak Forest Canal. Later, he meets TV host Terry Christian, whose dad once worked a local waterway

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie navigates the wintery Grand Union Canal while preparing for Christmas, and narrowly avoids flooding his boat in a tricky staircase lock

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon traces the path of the northern tropic around the globe, a journey in which he explores the environment, culture, history, politics and people of 18 countries. He begins in Baja California on Mexico's Pacific coast, crossing the country's rugged interior, before heading east to Cuba and ending the first leg in the Bahamas

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon continues to trace the path of the northern tropic around the globe, joining travellers on the coast of Western Sahara to partake in the extreme sport of kite surfing, and meeting activists who campaign for indigenous people's rights. He continues on through the world's largest minefield, takes a gruelling train ride across Mauritania and visits huge refugee camps in the Algerian desert

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The third leg of Simon's journey takes him from the banks of the Nile to the edge of the Indian Ocean in Oman. Along the way he is introduced to Nubians displaced by the formation of Lake Nasser in the 1960s and nomadic Bedouins who have been forced to move by a seven-year drought scientists believe is due to climate change. On Egypt's southern coast, he dives in pristine coral reefs, before seeing the unexpected sides of Saudi Arabian life

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie helps rescue a runaway narrowboat on the scenic Macclesfield Canal and cruises through autumn scenery toward Congleton to see a shipwreck up for sale

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: Frank survives an accident-prone journey to see Betty in hospital - and the medical staff soon find themselves wishing he'd arrived as a patient rather than a visitor. Michael Crawford, Michele Dotrice and Elisabeth Sladen star

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: Convinced he is a complete failure in life, Frank pays a visit to a psychiatrist. After a few minutes in his company, the unfortunate doctor finds himself in need of counselling. Classic comedy, guest starring Bernard Hepton, with Michael Crawford and Michele Dotrice

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: The job centre's new manager embarks on a quest to find Frank a job suited to his talents, despite the staff's long experience of failure. Comedy, starring Michael Crawford, Michele Dotrice and Edward Hardwicke

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team take on one of Derek's favourites - an Austin A35 - but its age ensures some hefty bills. Their second project is a quick flip - a 1999 BMW Z3

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: A Dutch DAF 66 - in vivid daffodil yellow - lands at the North Yorkshire showroom

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Paul and Charlie pick up a JZR three wheeler speed demon while Dave gets all nostalgic over a brace of Raleigh Choppers, a couple of Honda C90s and a rare Mark 1 Ford Granada

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus and Will want to buy a Scandinavian car and settle on a Saab, as they've never done one before. They would prefer a classic 99, but can only find an older 99 that was bought for a rally but can't go the distance. The hope is that it can somehow be improved

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus and Will want to buy a classic Rover, but have to go for a Honda-based 800 coupe. At only £650 it seems too good to be true - and when they turn up, they aren't completely sure whether to proceed

programme.title: Our Guy in India
programme.desc: Guy Martin takes a 1,000-mile journey across India to compete in a race called Rider Mania, the world's largest gathering of Royal Enfield motorbikes. In the first of two programmes, the daredevil rides with a 50-strong biker gang, visits Asia's biggest truck yard and finds himself completely out of his comfort zone

programme.title: Our Guy in India
programme.desc: Part two of two. Guy Martin visits Mumbai, where more skyscrapers are being built than anywhere else in the world, and clambers 833ft to the top of India's tallest building, the Imperial. Back on ground level, the daredevil visits a slum where a million people live in the space of one square mile, before experiencing a downpour on his Royal Enfield with the arrival of the monsoons. To avoid slow-moving and dangerous roads, he makes the final leg of his 1,000-mile journey by sea, on his way to compete in Rider Mania

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads out to pick up a pair of beautiful Swedish models - a Saab 96L and a Volvo P1800ES. Plus, a rare British AC Aceca comes up for sale

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates this documentary chronicling events leading up to the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbour on December 7, 1941, and the subsequent collapse of unprepared British forces before the advancing enemy in Singapore. Includes interviews with veterans from both sides

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: In Poland lies a forgotten Nazi complex which could have sheltered up to 27,244 people from Allied bombs, the purpose of which largely remains a mystery

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.desc: Robbie takes a detour to investigate a local UFO legend in Todmorden, and the UK's deepest canal lock springs a leak, causing trouble for the Naughty Lass

programme.title: Operation Gold Rush with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Felicity Aston and Dr Kevin Fong finally arrive at the end of their gruelling journey in Dawson City. They set up a traditional mining camp in the heart of the gold fields, before facing their next challenge. They have just five days to find gold in an area that has already been heavily mined, and can only use the back-breaking methods of the 19th-century early pioneers
